## PAGE 00289: Creating a validation dashboard

- page_id: `136712003`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712003/Creating+a+validation+dashboard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:04.270+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Metric Suites > Building validation-based metric definitions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you have several Metric tables where you calculate metrics using Metric Suites with validation-based metric definitions, you can display the validation results from these tables in a validation dashboard.

800450

To create a validation dashboard follow these steps:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a validation dashboard' space='MD2024x'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a validation dashboard' space='MD2024x'] .

##### Creating Metric Suites and Metric Tables to track in a validation dashboard

You can calculate model validation results in Metric Tables based on Metric Suites with validation-based metric definitions. Metrics calculated in these Metric Tables can later be displayed in a validation dashboard to track all validation results in one place. Follow the steps below to create Metric Suites and Metric Tables for a validation dashboard.

To create Metric Suites for a validation dashboard

1. Create a Package to store your Metric Suites.
2. Create a Profile Diagram and name it (e.g., *Metric Suites*).
3. In the Profile diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='MD2024x']Create a Metric Suite using the *BaseMetricSuite* for each validation rule you want to track.
4. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space='MD2024x'] for the Metric Suites created in the previous step. Example of a Metric Suite that tracks a validation ruleFor example, if you want to track the Unused Constraint Parameter validation rule, you can create a Metric Suite named *Unused Constrained Parameter* with the following validation-based metric definitions:Validation-based metric definition named*****Constrained Parameter* with the **Type** property set to **Integer**, **Validation Rule** property set to **Unused Constrained Parameter**, and the **Metric Type** property set to **Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Unused Constrained Parameter* with the **Type** property set to **Integer**, **Validation Rule** property set to **Unused Constrained Parameter**, and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Unused Constrained Parameter Percentage* with the **Type** property set to **Real**, **Validation Rule** property set to **Unused Constrained Parameter**, and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Percentage**.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a Metric Table' space='MD2024x'] for each Metric Suite created in step 3 and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Calculating metrics in the Metric Table' space='MD2024x'] .

##### Creating a validation dashboard

A validation dashboard is a convenient way to track model validation results in one place. It displays metric values from multiple Metric Tables created from validation-based Metric Suites.

To create a validation dashboard

1. Open the Profile Diagram in which you created Metric suites with validation based metric definitions (see step 2 in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a validation dashboard' space='MD2024x'] ).
2. In the Profile diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='MD2024x']Create a Metric Suite using the *BaseMetricSuite* and name it (e.g., Validation Dashboard ). [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_validation_dashboard_metric_suite.png']
3. Open the Specification window of the newly created Metric Suite (e.g., Validation Dashboard Metric Suite, as displayed above) and set the Is Abstract property to true .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space='MD2024x'] for the Metric Suite to define the structure of the dashboard. Each validation-based metric definition represents a column in the validation dashboard. Example of validation-based metric definitionsFor example, if you want to create a validation dashboard with the *Elements*, *Failed Element Quantity*, and *Failed Element Percentage* columns, create the following validation-based metric definitions:Validation-based metric definition named*****Elements* with the **Type** property set to **Integer** and the **Metric Type** property set to **Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Failed Element Quantity* with the **Type** property set to **Integer** and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Quantity**.Validation-based metric definition named *Failed Element Percentage* with the **Type** property set to **Real** and the **Metric Type** property set to **Failed Element Percentage**.
5. In the same Profile diagram, create a Generalization relationship from each Metric Suite that you created to track a validation rule (step 3 in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a validation dashboard' space='MD2024x'] ) to the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_metric_suites_to_track_validation_rules.png']
6. Link each validation-based metric definition that calculates the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard to the metric definition representing a specific validation dashboard column (see step 4), as described below:
  1. Open the Specification window of the validation-based metric definition that calculates the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard.
  2. Select the specification cell of the Redefined Property property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
  3. On the left side of the Select Property window, find the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2 and select its metric definition representing the validation dashboard column in which you want to display the metric.
  4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add the metric definition to the **Selected elements** area. Click **OK**. Example of linking a validation-based metric definitionLet's say you have the *Unused Constraint Parameter* Metric Suite with the *Constraint Parameter* validation-based metric definition that calculates the number of constraint parameters. If you want to display the number of constraint parametersin the *Elements* column of the validation dashboard, you must open the Specification window of the the*Constraint Parameter*validation-based metric definition, and select the *Elements* validation-based metric definition of the abstract Metric Suite (created in step 2) as the value of the **Redefined Property** property.
  5. Repeat steps a through d for each validation-based metric definition calculating the metric you want to display in the validation dashboard.
7. Build the validation dashboard:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a Metric Table' space='MD2024x'] that will be your validation dashboard.
  2. In the Metric table, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Metric Suite box.
  3. In the Select Metric Suite dialog, select the abstract Metric Suite created in step 2. Click OK .
  4. Open the Specification window of the Metric table.
  5. Select the specification cell of the Scope property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
  6. On the left side of the **Select Elements** dialog, select the Metric Tables you want to track in the validation dashboard. Selecting multiple elementsIf you cannot select multiple elements, click the **Multiple Selection** button on the bottom of the **Select Elements** dialog.
  7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to add the selected Metric tables to the **Selected elements** area. Click **OK**.
  8. In the Specification widow of the Metric Table, select the specification cell of the Query property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
  9. On the left side of the **Query** dialog, select **Create operation** and click the **Operation from Model** operation on the right side of the dialog. Enabling the Expert modeThe **Operation from Model** operation is only available in the Expert mode. To enable the Expert mode, click the **Expert** button on the bottom left corner of the **Query** dialog.
  10. In the **Select Opaque Behavior** dialog, select the *LatestMetricInstances* Opaque Behavior. Click **OK**.
  11. On the left side of the Query dialog, select Element = unspecified located under the LatestMetricInstances1 operation and click the Reset button. [ATTACHMENT filename='removing_argument_of_parameter.png']
  12. On the right side of the Query dialog, select the Contextual Variable operation in the Other operation group and click OK .
  13. Close the Specification window of the Metric Table.

The latest instances from the specified Metric Tables are now collected and displayed in your validation dashboard, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This sample validation dashboard tracks the latest metrics from three Metric Tables.

## PAGE 00290: Creating an alternative flow

- page_id: `136711423`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711423/Creating+an+alternative+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:39.183+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['alternative-condition', 'alternative-flow-step']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879536389

879536391

879536390

To create an alternative flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. Select the basic flow step you want to create an alternative flow for.
3. In the Alternative Flow tab, click the Add new button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_alternative_flow.png']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the **Add New Alternative Condition**command or press ALT+R to add a new alternative condition. A new alternative condition is created together with the alternative flow step. Type the name of the alternative condition. The alternative condition for the basic flow is created.
  - Select the Add New Alternative Flow Step command. Press ALT+ENTER to add a new alternative flow step. If there was an alternative condition created in the alternative flow already, a new alternative flow step is created for the selected alternative condition. If in the alternative flow there was no alternative condition, a new alternative flow step is created together with a new alternative condition. Now you can type the name of the new alternative flow step.

Conditions and steps of the alternative flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of conditions in the alternative flow or the order of steps in a particular condition. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

879536388

The shortcut menu opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_alternative_flow.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating an alternative flow" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Do one of the following:</p></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Add New Alternative Condition </strong>command or press ALT+R to add a new alternative condition. A new alternative condition is created together with the alternative flow step. Type the name of the alternative condition. The alternative condition for the basic flow is created.</p></li><li>Select the <strong>Add New Alternative Flow Step </strong>command. Press ALT+ENTER to add a new alternative flow step. If there was an alternative condition created in the alternative flow already, a new alternative flow step is created for the selected alternative condition. If in the alternative flow there was no alternative condition, a new alternative flow step is created together with a new alternative condition. Now you can type the name of the new alternative flow step.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Conditions and steps of the alternative flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_selected_row_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and Down <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_selected_row.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> buttons to change the order of conditions in the alternative flow or the order of steps in a particular condition. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08d48bca-7a36-46cb-a6dc-8a7cef6561fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879536388</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710781 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00291: Creating an aspect diagram

- page_id: `136710781`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710781/Creating+an+aspect+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:00.818+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581406002

581406004

581406003

Before creating an aspect diagram, the following conditions must be met:

- The custom aspects are created in the project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='MD2024x']>]]> If you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x'] installed, the predefined aspect diagrams already exist in your project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finding predefined diagram aspects' space='MD2024x']Predefined diagram aspects >>
- The Use Aspects option is enabled. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option' space='MD2024x']How to enable the diagram aspects option >>

Creating a new aspect diagram is similar to creating any other diagram. Every way to create a diagram is described here: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']. The difference is that aspect [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] are automatically collected under the separate **Aspect Diagrams** group of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']**Create Diagram** dialog. If you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x'] installed, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024x'] with custom and predefined aspects are collected under the same **Aspect Diagrams** group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Aspect Diagram group, highlighted in orange, with predefined and custom UML and SysML aspect diagrams. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581406001

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample models that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x']. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710363 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00292: Creating an empty Instance Table

- page_id: `136710363`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710363/Creating+an+empty+Instance+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:36.367+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an empty Instance Table

1. In the Containment tree, select an element to be the owner of the Instance Table.
2. Do one of the following: The newly created Instance Table opens on the right side of the application window.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - On the main toolbars, click the Create Diagram button. Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - Press Ctrl+N. Type “ins” and press Enter.
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Other Diagrams > Instance Table .

- If the default name of the Instance Table is not appropriate, type a new one directly in the Containment tree.
- If the owner of the Instance Table is also a classifier, it becomes the default value of the Classifier box in this Instance Table. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_instance_table_with_default_classifier.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an empty Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element to be the owner of the Instance Table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>On the main toolbars, click the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> button. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>Press Ctrl+N. Type “ins” and press Enter.</li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Other </strong><strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance Table</strong>.</li></ul>The newly created Instance Table opens on the right side of the application window.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4864ee64-0471-41e8-b42e-dcde36bf0813"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the default name of the Instance Table is not appropriate, type a new one directly in the Containment tree.</li><li>If the owner of the Instance Table is also a classifier, it becomes the default value of the Classifier box in this Instance Table.<br /> <ac:image ac:title="Owner of the Instance Table becomes classifier by default" ac:alt="Owner of the Instance Table becomes classifier by default"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_instance_table_with_default_classifier.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating an empty Instance Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711425 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00294: Creating an exceptional flow

- page_id: `136711425`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711425/Creating+an+exceptional+flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:17:34.717+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['exception-type', 'exception-flow-step']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879686455

879686457

879686456

INLINE

To create an exceptional flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. In the use case scenario, select the basic flow step you want to create an exceptional flow for.
3. In the Exceptional Flow tab, click the Add New button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the **Add New Exception Type**command or press Alt+T. The drop down list opens. Type the name of a new exception type. The exception type is created (a new class is created). The created exception type contains an unnamed exceptional flow step.
  - Select the **Add New Exceptional Flow Step**command or press Alt+Enter. If there was an exceptional type created in the exceptional flow already, a new exceptional flow step is created for the selected exception type. If there was no exception type in the alternative flow, a new exceptional flow step is created together with a new exception type. Now you can type the name of the new exceptional flow step.

Types and steps of the exceptional flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of types in the exceptional flow or the order of steps in the particular type. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

879686454

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']

The shortcut menu opens.</span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_exceptional_flow.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><p>Do one of the following:</p></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>Select the <strong>Add New Exception Type </strong>command or press Alt+T. The drop down list opens. Type the name of a new exception type. The exception type is created (a new class is created). The created exception type contains an unnamed exceptional flow step.</p></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Add New Exceptional Flow Step </strong>command or press Alt+Enter. If there was an exceptional type created in the exceptional flow already, a new exceptional flow step is created for the selected exception type. If there was no exception type in the alternative flow, a new exceptional flow step is created together with a new exception type. Now you can type the name of the new exceptional flow step.</p></li></ul></li></ul><p><span>Types and steps of the exceptional flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_selected_row_button.png" /></ac:image> and Down <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_selected_row.png" /></ac:image>  buttons to change the order of types in the exceptional flow or the order of steps in the particular type. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19245a41-c7e4-45f2-9339-15d4764e20ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879686454</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713039 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00295: Creating and managing categories

- page_id: `136713039`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713039/Creating+and+managing+categories
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:14.653+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Using categories and grouping projects into them, you can organize the structure of your server projects' repository according to your needs.

By default, there is a single category *Uncategorized*, but you can create as many categories as you need. A project can be created directly in a selected category and later easily moved to another one. The category can be created in advance or while [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024x']. Categories can be renamed and removed. Also, they can have sub-categories.

The TWCloud project categories in the modeling tool dialogs will be hidden if a user does not have the *Manage Categories* or*Read-Only*permission to at least one project inside of these categories.

Read the following to learn how

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing categories' space='MD2024x']

Categories can also be managed via the Resource application. Learn more about this in .

You need to have the Manage categories permission assigned to the Global scope to be able to create categories.

createTo create a category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Be sure the Categorized View is turned on in the dialog. If it is not, click [ATTACHMENT filename='categorized_view.png'] on the toolbar of the dialog.
3. Choose one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click anywhere in the project list area and then select [ATTACHMENT filename='create_category.png'] Create Category .
4. After the **Create Category** dialog opens, select a **Parent** category and type a new category name. Select an existing category as a **Parent** category to nest a new category within.Select a *Root* node as a **Parent** category to create a new top-level category. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click OK to confirm the creation. The category is created. You can find it in the project list ordered alphabetically.

renameTo rename a category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select a category or a project that belongs to the category you want to rename and then do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='rename_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Rename Category**. The *Uncategorized* category cannot be renamed*.*
3. When the Rename Category dialog opens, type a new category name.
4. Click OK . The category is renamed.

moveTo move a project to another category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select the project you want to move and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='move_to_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [ATTACHMENT filename='move_to_category.png'] Move to Category .
3. When the**Move to Category** dialog opens, select the category to which you want to move the project.
  - To create a new category, select the Create Category button in the Move to Category dialog: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click OK . The project appears in the selected category.

- You need to have the Manage Resources permission to delete the selected category.
- You need to have the Read Resources/List Resources/List All Resources and Remove Resources permissions to delete all the resources within the selected category.

removeTo remove the category

1. From the Collaborate menu, choose Projects . The Manage Projects dialogs opens.
2. Select the category or the project that belongs to the category you want to remove and then do either:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_category.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog.
  - Right-click the selection and then select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Remove Category**. The *Uncategorized* category cannot be removed*.*
3. Click Yes to confirm your decision. The category is removed from the server repository together with all the resources in it .

Empty categories are by default shown in the **Manage Projects** and **Open Server Project** dialogs, but you can simply hide them.

emptyTo hide empty categories

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show_empty_categories.png'] on the toolbar of the open dialog. Click the button once more to show the empty categories again.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Using categories and grouping projects into them, you can organize the structure of your server projects' repository according to your <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0451c6b3-39ec-4d32-8c98-6f3a74b67cbe">needs</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p>By default, there is a single category <em>Uncategorized</em>, but you can create as many categories as you need. A project can be created directly in a selected category and later easily moved to another one. The category can be created in advance or while <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[adding the project to the server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Categories can be renamed and removed. Also, they can have sub-categories. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ebd8ec1-5ec1-4393-aa4e-8c6e1015f947"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The TWCloud project categories in the modeling tool dialogs will be hidden if a user does not have the <em>Manage Categories</em> o</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">r </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Read-Only</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> permission to at least one project inside of these categories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Read the following to learn how</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To create a single category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="rename"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To rename a category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="move"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To move a project to another category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To remove the category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To hide empty categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ac:link></li></ul><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edc918a1-2b41-4d4d-9063-2ec9df702777"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Categories can also be managed via the Resource application. Learn more about this in <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud User Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ff185c0-7342-4825-a23a-92d0fc860495"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have the Manage categories permission assigned to the Global scope to be able to create categories.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89343c45-2424-4020-a677-9d1171c262b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Be sure the Categorized View is turned on in the dialog. If it is not, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="categorized_view.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the dialog.</li><li>Choose one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li>Right-click anywhere in the project list area and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">After the <strong>Create Category</strong> dialog opens, select a <strong>Parent</strong> category and type a new category name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae28100a-bc48-428c-9d9f-3fdea3dc828a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Select an existing category as a <strong>Parent</strong> category to nest a new category within.</li><li>Select a <em>Root</em> node as a <strong>Parent</strong> category to create a new top-level category. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="root_node_nested_categories_TWC.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the creation.<br />The category is created. You can find it in the project list ordered alphabetically.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c47db4-7792-4b2e-ba26-9b08ad30d3b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rename</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To rename a category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select a category or a project that belongs to the category you want to rename and then do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li><p>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Rename Category</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e2a3933-639d-4309-adb3-bdf3a1c06c89"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>Uncategorized</em> category cannot be renamed<em>.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Rename Category</strong> dialog opens, type a new category name.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The category is renamed.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3282daa0-3f5e-413b-b9f6-fd60f87c6991"><ac:parameter ac:name="">move</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To move a project to another category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select the project you want to move and do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_to_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_to_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Move to Category</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p>When the<strong> Move to Category</strong> dialog opens, select the category to which you want to move the project.</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a new category, select the</span><strong> Create Category</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button in the </span><strong>Move to Category</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_category_button_in_move_to_category_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing categories" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The project appears in the selected category.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0d68d02-ea3c-49ac-95f7-87c9b7177417"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to have the Manage Resources permission to delete the selected category.</li><li>You need to have the Read Resources/List Resources/List All Resources and Remove Resources permissions to delete all the resources within the selected category.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05ffedaf-49fc-4840-855d-647482274ec4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove the category</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, choose <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialogs opens.</li><li>Select the category or the project that belongs to the category you want to remove and then do either:<ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog.</li><li><p>Right-click the selection and then select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_category.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Remove Category</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="938889ef-613c-4d9a-9dc7-85382930ad60"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>Uncategorized</em> category cannot be removed<em>.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm your decision.<br />The category is removed from the server repository <strong>together with all the resources in it</strong>. </li></ol><p> </p><p>Empty categories are by default shown in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> and <strong>Open Server Project</strong> dialogs, but you can simply hide them.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab80dfe7-3d1a-4112-93d1-9abad07af7f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To hide empty categories</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_empty_categories.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the open dialog. Click the button once more to show the empty categories again.<br /><br /></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710536 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00296: Creating and managing Legend Items

- page_id: `136710536`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710536/Creating+and+managing+Legend+Items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:44.202+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695897

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695899

1293695896

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

1293695898

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024x'], you must create and specify its Legend Items. Legend Itemsdefine different styles used in a diagram or table in a Legend.

##### Creating Legend Items

You can create Legend Items directly from a Legend shape or in the Specification window of a Legend. If you are working with a Legend in a symbol diagram, you can use both methods. However, if you are working with a Legend in a table, you can create Legend Items only in the Specification window of the Legend.

To create a Legend Item from a Legend shape

1. In a diagram, select the shape of the Legend for which you want to create a Legend Item.
2. Click the smart manipulator on the top right corner of the Legend shape, highlighted in the figure below, and select **Create Legend Item**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of the newly created Legend Item, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024x'].
4. Close the Specification window.

The newly created Legend Item is displayed in the Legend shape and applied to diagram symbols according to the properties you specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='MD2024x']**Applied To** property group.

To create a Legend Item in the Specification window of a Legend

1. Open the Specification window of the Legend for which you want to create a Legend Item by doing one of the following:
  - In a symbol diagram, double-click the shape of the desired Legend.
  - In a table, hover the mouse pointer over the desired Legend, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and select Specification .
2. Click the Legend Items group on the left side of the Specification window.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the open Specification window of the newly created Legend Item, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='MD2024x'].
5. Close the Specification window.

The newly created Legend Item is displayed in the Legend and applied to diagram symbols or table rows according to the properties you specified in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='MD2024x']**Applied To** property group.

Alternatively, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024x'].

##### Managing Legend Items

The **Legend Items** group in the Specification window of a Legend allows you to create new Legend Items and modify or reorder the existing ones.

To access the Legend Item management area

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Click the Legend Items group on the left side of the Specification window.

The Legend Item management area is displayed on the right side of the Specification window. The following table explains how to use the buttons found there to create and modify Legend Items.

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Up | Select a Legend Item and click the button to move the Legend Item one position up in the list. |
| Down | Select a Legend Item and click the button to move the Legend Item one position down in the list. |
| Create | Click the button to create a Legend Item. The Specification window of the newly created Legend Item opens automatically, allowing you to specify its properties. |
| Clone | Select a Legend Item and click the button to create its copy. The Specification window of the newly created Legend Item clone opens automatically, allowing you to modify its properties. By default, the clone inherits all property values of the Legend Item from which it was cloned. |
| Delete | Select a Legend Item and click the button to remove it. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Legend Item management area in the Specification window of a Legend.

#### NOTE: Alternative method to manage Legend Items

Alternative method to manage Legend Items

You can also manage Legend Items in the **Legend Items** property specification cell:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Select the specification cell of the Legend Items property.
3. Complete one of the following actions:
  - To create a Legend Item, click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] .
  - To remove a Legend Item, select the Legend Item you want to remove and click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png'] .
  - To reorder Legend Items, click [ATTACHMENT filename='order.png'] and in the open dialog use the Up and Down buttons to change the position of the selected Legend Item.

580309733

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715034 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00297: Creating and specifying derived properties

- page_id: `136715034`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715034/Creating+and+specifying+derived+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:01.166+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

185690227

185690230

185690228

To be included in a MagicDraw project as a property extending a UML element, an attribute must be owned by a DSL customization element and stereotyped as «derivedPropertySpecification».

To create and specify a derived property

1. Select the customization element for which you want to create a derived property. The derived property will be created for the stereotype or metaclass, defined as a customization target in the selected customization element specification.
2. Select **Create Element** > **Derived Property Specification** from the shortcut menu. The attribute is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Type the attribute name. Your Model Browser should look like this at this stage: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The attribute’s name corresponds to the name of the derived property.Type derived property names in camel case (i.e.,*realizingActivities*).A property name defined in camel case will be displayed in separate words with the first word capitalized. For example, realizingActivities will be changed to *Realizing Activities* (visible in the user interface).
4. Edit property values specifying the derived property in the attribute's Specification window. All properties are specified in the general specification pane. Some are also specified in the **Tags** specification pane. For detailed information, see the properties [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space='MD2024x'].

If you do not see the newly created derived property in the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], please read the following:

- In the element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , the specified derived property is visible by default only in the All properties mode. First, try switching to this mode. Also try changing the mode in which you want the derived property to be visible (click the Customize button).
- If you have specified the derived property for the element (with the Used UML Properties property of DSL customization specified) you must add the newly created derived property to the element’s visible properties list. For detailed information, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Derived properties visibility' space='MD2024x'] .

description_tableThe following table describes the properties specified for the derived property.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | The name of the property.This name will be added and visible in the specification of the element whose type is defined as a customization target. |
| Type | The type of the derived property values. You may set a model element type or a data type (e.g., string, boolean, orintegerfrom the UML Standard Profile) as a property value.To display classes and components as property values, select the classifier as a type for the derived property value. |
| Multiplicity | The multiplicity of the derived property values.If the multiplicity is “0..1”, the derived property value will be shown as single.If the multiplicity is “0..*”, the derived property values will be shown as collection. |
| Is Read Only | If true, the derived property is read-only. You cannot edit the result elements for the derived property value.If false, the property is read-write. You can edit the result elements for the derived property according to the expressions defined. |
| Is Ordered | If true, the property values are always displayed in the same order.If false, the property values are displayed in nonpredictable order. |
| Is Unique | If true, the property values are unique. The same element is displayed only once.If false, the property values may be displayed more than once. |
| Expression | One or more expressions of the derived property.An expression defines the criterion for selecting result elements.There are ten possible expression types:Metachain navigation (indirect relations through chains of properties)FindUnionExcludeFilterVarious scriptsImplied RelationType TestProperty TestOperation from Model The criterion for selecting the result elements can also be any combination of the expression types above.For the detailed information, see .Only one Simple Navigation operation criterion can be assigned to the expression of the derived property. |
| Documentation | The text displayed as the derived property description in the element’s and Properties panel. |
| customizationTarget | The specified derived property will be added to one or more element types (stereotypes and/ or metaclasses).The expression calculations begin from the element type of the customization target. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of the derived property

185690225

**Related pages:**

- Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- Using Customization Data
- Creating Customization Data
- Defining Expressions

Project template is a customized project pattern. It serves as a starting point for creating a new project in a predefined format.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710906 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00299: Creating Class Elements

- page_id: `136710906`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710906/Creating+Class+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:06.199+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] is drawn as a solid-outline rectangle with three compartments separated by horizontal lines. The top name compartment holds the class name and other general properties of the Class (including [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']); the middle list compartment holds a list of properties; the bottom list compartment holds a list of operations. The property and operation compartments are optional, so you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes' space='MD2024x'] them.

To create a class element

1. On the left side of your model, find the available options under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] toolbar and click on Class.
2. You will see an arrow for a drop-down menu which you can click if you wish to create a different kind of Class element.
3. In any case, either At this point, you should have a Class element in your Class diagram, as shown in figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.22.38 PM.png']
  1. Click on the element and then click anywhere on your diagram pane, or
  2. Drag and drop, the element you clicked on, anywhere on your diagram pane.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710777 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00300: Creating custom aspects

- page_id: `136710777`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710777/Creating+custom+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:00.717+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581366052

581366054

581366053

You can define your own diagram aspects that you want to use in the project. You can create custom aspects in the same way you create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']. The procedures below describe how to create a custom diagram aspect, e.g., Air, in two different ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='MD2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom aspects' space='MD2024x']

##### createpd

To create a new aspect on the Profile Diagram pane

1. Open or create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024x'] .
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024x'] , click the Stereotype button, place it on the diagram pane, and type its name, e.g., Air.
3. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option.
4. Expand packages *UML Standard Profile* > *MagicDraw Profile*, and drag the *aspect* Stereotype on the same diagram pane.
5. Create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2024x'] relationship from the newly created Stereotype to the *aspect* Stereotype. 
The new aspect is created, and can be used in the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The creation of the new diagram aspect on the Profile Diagram pane.

##### createct

To create a new aspect in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select either a Profile or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x']
2. From its shortcut menu , click the Create Element button.
3. In the Create Element dialog, select Stereotype, and type its name, e.g., Air.
4. Open its Specification window, select the All properties mode, and find the Base Classifier property.
5. Select the value cell of the Base Classifier property, click the Edit button.
6. In the Select Stereotype dialog, clear the Apply Filter check box.
7. In the Tree tab, expand packages *UML Standard Profile* > *MagicDraw Profile*, and double click to select the *aspect* Stereotype.
8. Click **OK** and **Close**. The new aspect is created, and can be used in the project. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The creation of the new diagram aspect in the Containment tree.

After the custom stereotype is created in the model, the Composite Structure diagram with the custom aspect applied can be found in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']**Create Diagram** dialog under the **Aspect Diagrams** group when creating a new aspect diagram. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an aspect diagram' space='MD2024x']>]]>

581366051

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample models that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x']. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dff08587-fdbc-4b6d-b45e-7c4b86cebc10"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581366052</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef5bb9e9-1afd-4ece-8c8a-651825e18df8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581366054</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="721073be-f59d-451a-834a-974a91852fdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581366053</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can define your own diagram aspects that you want to use in the project. You can create custom aspects in the same way you create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The procedures below describe how to create a custom diagram aspect, e.g., Air, in two different ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="createpd"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[On the Profile Diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="createct"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[In the Containment tree.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /></ac:link></li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9c84dc7-ead0-4f1c-93b6-c41ee48f4463"><ac:parameter ac:name="">createpd</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></h3><p>To create a new aspect on the Profile Diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>Open or create the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Profile diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Profile Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click the <strong>Stereotype</strong> button, place it on the diagram pane, and type its name, e.g., Air.</li><li><span>In the Containment tree, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>,</span> and select the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option.</span></li><li><span>Expand packages <em>UML Standard Profile</em> &gt; <em>MagicDraw Profile</em>, and drag the <em>aspect</em> Stereotype on the same diagram pane.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ac:link> relationship from the newly created Stereotype to the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><em>aspect</em> Stereotype</span>.<br />The new aspect is created, and can be used in the project.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_custom_aspect_on_profile_diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The creation of the new diagram aspect on the Profile Diagram pane. <br /><br /></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa38b1af-42c9-4cd3-8dcd-e749709b03f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">createct</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></h3><p>To create a new aspect in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select either a Profile or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>From its shortcut menu<strong>, </strong>click the <strong>Create Element</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select <strong>Stereotype,</strong> and type its name, e.g., Air.</li><li>Open its Specification window, select the <strong>All</strong> properties mode, and find the <strong>Base Classifier</strong> property.</li><li>Select the value cell of the <strong>Base Classifier</strong> property, click the <strong>Edit</strong> button.</li><li>In the Select Stereotype dialog, clear the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box.</li><li>In the Tree tab, <span>expand packages <em>UML Standard Profile</em> &gt; <em>MagicDraw Profile</em>, and double click to select the <em>aspect</em> Stereotype.</span></li><li><span>Click <strong>OK</strong> and <strong>Close</strong>.</span><span> </span><br /><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The new aspect is created, and can be used in the project.</span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_custom_aspect_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating custom aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span><h6 style="text-align: left;">The creation of the new diagram aspect in the Containment tree.</h6></li></ol><p>After the custom stereotype is created in the model, the Composite Structure diagram with the custom aspect applied can be found in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:link-body><strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> under the <strong>Aspect Diagrams</strong> group when creating a new aspect diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating an aspect diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a new aspect diagram >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dc2c846-5669-402c-b0c7-65a44454b58e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581366051</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample models that comes with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715360 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00301: Creating custom drag and drop handlers

- page_id: `136715360`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715360/Creating+custom+drag+and+drop+handlers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:24.962+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MagicDraw itself has many default drag and drop handlers for doing various things. Such handlers consist of :

- Dropping elements from various UI components(trees, specification windows, etc.) diagram or its presentation elements.
- Dropping external application items onto trees or diagrams.

Default drag and drop handlers for example create new presentation elements, change the properties of elements, external application image drop on diagram creates an image and etc. Same is true if presentation elements are dropped on top of other presentation elements.

API allows to register a custom drag and drop handler for doing some specific things using drag and drop mechanism.

OpenAPI drag and drop handler registrations:

- Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDropDiagramHandlerFactory.register(* [com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory.html)) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping elements from browser(trees) into diagram.
- Use [com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.register(](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.html#register(com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory))[com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomDragAndDropHandlerFactory.html)[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/dnd/CustomShapeMoveHandlerFactory.html#register-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.CustomDragAndDropHandler-) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping one presentation element on other presentation element in the same diagram
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.BrowserTabTreeDragAndDropHandlerRegistry#register ( com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.BrowserTabTreeDragAndDropHandlerFactory ) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping external application items into the browser tree nodes(elements).
- Use com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.DiagramTransferableDragAndDropHandlerRegistry#register ( com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dnd.DiagramTransferableDragAndDropHandlerFactory ) to register a factory which creates a handler for dropping external application items into the diagram.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory> \openapi\examples\customdraganddrop

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709493 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00302: Creating custom project templates

- page_id: `136709493`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709493/Creating+custom+project+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:52.031+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Creating and using project templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create your custom project template that appears in the **New Project** dialog.

Each project template is saved in a separate folder in *<MagicDraw installation directory>\templates*. The template folder should consist of the following files:

- A MagicDraw project file containing predefined initial project data.
- An .xml file describing project template data. See the following figure wherein all customizable template data are depicted.
- An image of a dialog title bar icon that is displayed in the title bar of the New Project dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 80 x 80 pixels. Recommended image file formats are .gif or .png .
- An image of a template icon that is displayed in a particular project template category of the New Project dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 24 x 24 pixels. Recommended image file formats are .gif or .png .

Folder name, MagicDraw project file name and *.xml* file name must be identical.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

While creating a project template, you can also create a category wherein your template can be located. If you need to have several project templates under the same category, in the .xml file, define the same category name for these templates. 
 
 To create a custom project template

1. If MagicDraw is running, close it.
2. In < MagicDraw installation directory>\templates create a new folder for your template files.
3. Paste a MagicDraw project file and icon image files to the created folder. The project file name should be the same as the folder name.
4. In the same folder, create an .xml file and name it the same name as a folder name. The .xml file content should be as follows: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> 
<custom_template> 
<icon>Project_Template_Icon.gif</icon> 
<iconLabel>Projecttemplatename</iconLabel> 
<perspective>MagicDrawperspectivename</perspective> 
<tooltip>Projecttemplatetooltiptext</tooltip> 
<banner_title>Titlebarname</banner_title> 
<banner_description>Projecttemplatedescription</banner_description> 
<category>Templatecategoryname</category> 
<banner_icon>Title_Bar_Icon.png</banner_icon> 
<cloneModules/> 
</custom_template>
5. Save the file and start MagicDraw. Your created project template is displayed in the **New Project** dialog in the category defined by you. Select your template and create a new project. The easiest way to create a custom template is to copy and paste the **Guide to UML****Diagrams****Project** folder with the different name and edit its inner files. This folder comes with the default MagicDraw installation and is located in *<MagicDraw installation directory>\templates*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

See the following figure wherein all customizable template data are depicted.</li><li>An image of a dialog title bar icon that is displayed in the title bar of the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 80 x 80 pixels. Recommended image file formats are <em>.gif</em> or <em>.png</em>.</li><li>An image of a template icon that is displayed in a particular project template category of the <strong>New</strong> <strong>Project</strong> dialog. The recommended maximum image size is 24 x 24 pixels. Recommended image file formats are <em>.gif</em> or <em>.png</em>.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="948270d5-a602-4f5f-b37a-d7ad20535f01"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Folder name, MagicDraw project file name and <em>.xml</em> file name must be identical.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:title="Customizable data for project templates" ac:alt="Customizable data for project templates"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizable_data_project_template.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating custom project templates" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>While creating a project template, you can also create a category wherein your template can be located. The .xml file content should be as follows:<br /><pre>&lt;?xml version=&quot;1.0&quot; encoding=&quot;UTF-8&quot;?&gt;<br />&lt;custom_template&gt;<br />&lt;icon&gt;Project_Template_Icon.gif&lt;/icon&gt;<br />&lt;iconLabel&gt;Projecttemplatename&lt;/iconLabel&gt;<br />&lt;perspective&gt;MagicDrawperspectivename&lt;/perspective&gt;<br class="atl-forced-newline" />&lt;tooltip&gt;Projecttemplatetooltiptext&lt;/tooltip&gt;<br />&lt;banner_title&gt;Titlebarname&lt;/banner_title&gt;<br />&lt;banner_description&gt;Projecttemplatedescription&lt;/banner_description&gt;<br />&lt;category&gt;Templatecategoryname&lt;/category&gt;<br />&lt;banner_icon&gt;Title_Bar_Icon.png&lt;/banner_icon&gt;<br />&lt;cloneModules/&gt;<br />&lt;/custom_template&gt;</pre><p> </p></li><li><p>Save the file and start MagicDraw. Your created project template is displayed in the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog in the category defined by you. Select your template and create a new project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="894dc761-0f00-44bb-810c-5baf55900a9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The easiest way to create a custom template is to copy and paste the <strong>Guide to UML</strong><strong> Diagrams</strong><strong> Project</strong> folder with the different name and edit its inner files.
