<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710580 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00319: Creating Legends automatically

- page_id: `136710580`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710580/Creating+Legends+automatically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:46.084+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Legends
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1287060642

1287060644

143669993

4

1287060643

You can create Legends automatically by extracting them from model elements. Additionally, you can automatically add new Legend items directly from the smart manipulator of the Legend already displayed on your diagram.

##### Extracting Legends

You can create a Legend with the selected Legend items automatically. Legends can be extracted either from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024x'] or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024x'].

Extracting from tagsTo extract Legends automatically from the applied stereotype tags typed by Boolean or Enumeration

1. Do one of the following:
  - in an open diagram, right-click the element shape and select Tools > Extract Legends in the shortcut menu.
  - in the Model Browser, right-click the actual element and select Tools > Extract Legends in the shortcut menu.
  - in the open table, right-click the row representing the actual element and select [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Extract Legends From Row Type .
2. In the open Extract Legends dialog, select the Legend representing the stereotype tag to generate a Legend from. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

Extracting from element symbols

1. Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Extract Legends .
3. Extract Legends dialog In the open Extract Legends dialog, select the Legends you want to create automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_legends_from_elements.png']
4. Click Extract .

##### Extracting Legend items

You can add the selected Legend items automatically from the smart manipulator of the Legend shape displayed on the diagram pane.

To extract the selected Legend items automatically

1. Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend shape, click Auto-create Legend items .
3. In the open **Auto-create Legend Items** dialog, select the specific Legend item(s) to extract. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click Extract .

The selected Legend items are automatically displayed in the Legend shape, and diagram symbols are adorned accordingly.

When extracting Legends or Legend items, you can specify the following options:

- Select Create Adornments to automatically set a Fill Color for element shapes and Pen Color for paths.
- Select Connectors by Ports to automatically create Legends for Connectors depending on the selected Ports Legend(s) ( Stereotypes of Port Types , Stereotypes of Ports , and/or Port Types ).

##### Legend extraction mechanism

Legends listed in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends automatically' space='MD2024x']** dialog are generated automatically according to the types of elements displayed as symbols on the diagram pane.

| Legend | Legend items |
| --- | --- |
| Element Types | element types (metaclasses and stereotypes) displayed on a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Port Types | stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Part Types | stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram |
| Stereotypes of Ports | stereotypes of ports displayed in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Parts | stereotypes of parts displayed in a diagram. |
| Stereotypes of Connectors | stereotypes of connectors displayed in a diagram. |
| Port Types | classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram. |
| Part Types | classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Connector | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed connectors in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Activity Edge | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed activity edges in a diagram. |
| Flowing Items of Association | conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed associations in a diagram. |
| Name of applied stereotype tag typed by enumeration | enumeration literals owned by the applied stereotype tag type. |
| Name of applied stereotype tag typed by boolean | true and false values. |

##### **Example**

Let's say that you want to extract the Legend identifying different **Risk** property values of Requirements in your model. To do this, complete the following steps:

1. Open the Requirement Diagram in which you want to create the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values.
2. Right-click the shape of any Requirement and select Tools > Extract Legends OR in the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > Extract Legends .
3. In the open dialog, select the Risk Legend, as shown in the figure below.
4. Click OK .

The *Risk* Legend is automatically displayed and applied to the Requirements Diagram. The name of the element tag is used as the Legend name, and every possible **Risk** property value creates an individual Legend Item.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The process of creating the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values in a Requirement Diagram.

579958103

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the *Legends* sample model that comes with SysML plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='legends.mdzip'].*
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML directory .

1287060641

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space='MD2024x']

Legends</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"> in the shortcut menu.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c">in the Model Browser, right-click the actual element and select </ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c">Tools</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c">Extract Legends</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8515c237-0f1e-459d-868e-cf82647f2a2c"> in the shortcut menu.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">in the open table, right-click the row representing the actual element and select </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">Extract Legends From Row Type</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c11ae435-7f4a-455e-ac32-fb8502564b98">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li><li>In the open <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Extract Legends</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog, select the Legend representing the stereotype tag to generate a Legend from. </span><br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_legends_from_tags.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><div><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6e859cb-26bb-4567-85ef-6f9e352559bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Extracting from element symbols</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To extract Legends automatically from the elements displayed on a diagram</div><div><hr /></div><div><ol><li>Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Extract Legends</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li><li><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18272490-f32d-4d72-977e-a3bfd43ad3c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Extract Legends dialog</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the open </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Extract Legends</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog, select the Legends you want to create <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de714e0b-2969-4ef9-894b-1d81554f8f85">automatically</ac:inline-comment-marker>. </span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_legends_from_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Extract</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Extracting Legend items</h3><p>You can add the selected Legend items automatically from the smart manipulator of the Legend shape displayed on the diagram pane. </p><p><br /></p><p>To extract the selected Legend items automatically</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Open a diagram in which you want to create a Legend.</li><li>In the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend shape, click <strong>Auto-create Legend items</strong>. </li><li><p>In the open <strong>Auto-create Legend Items</strong> dialog, select the specific Legend item(s) to extract.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="auto_create_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click<strong> Extract</strong>.</li></ol><p>The selected Legend items are automatically displayed in the Legend shape, and diagram symbols are adorned accordingly.</p><p><br /></p><p>When extracting Legends or Legend items, you can specify the following options:</p><ul><li>Select <strong>Create Adornments</strong> to automatically set a Fill Color for element shapes and Pen Color for paths.</li><li>Select <strong>Connectors by Ports</strong> to automatically create Legends for Connectors depending on the selected Ports Legend(s) (<strong>Stereotypes of Port Types</strong>, <strong>Stereotypes of Ports</strong>, and/or<strong> Port Types</strong>).</li></ul></div><h3>Legend extraction mechanism</h3><p>Legends listed in the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Extract Legends dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Legends]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ac:link></strong> dialog are generated automatically according to the types of elements displayed as symbols on the diagram pane.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 70.6528%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 29.9922%;" /><col style="width: 70.0078%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Legend</th><th>Legend items</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Element Types</strong></td><td>element types (metaclasses and stereotypes) displayed on a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Stereotypes of Port Types</strong></td><td>stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Stereotypes of Part Types</strong></td><td>stereotypes of classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Stereotypes of Ports</strong></td><td colspan="1">stereotypes of ports displayed in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Stereotypes of Parts</strong></td><td colspan="1">stereotypes of parts displayed in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Stereotypes of Connectors</strong></td><td colspan="1">stereotypes of connectors displayed in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Port Types</strong></td><td colspan="1">classifiers used as displayed port types in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Part Types</strong></td><td colspan="1">classifiers used as displayed property types in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Flowing Items of Connector</strong></td><td colspan="1">conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed connectors in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Flowing Items of Activity Edge</strong></td><td colspan="1">conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed activity edges in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Flowing Items of Association</strong></td><td colspan="1">conveyed classifiers of item flows realized by displayed associations in a diagram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name of applied stereotype tag typed by enumeration</span></strong></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="da7d788e-009f-4d3d-a253-97511512b831"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53fd64ee-e50c-474b-ba90-9b0c1405b58a"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">enumeration literals owned by the applied stereotype tag type. </span></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name of applied stereotype tag typed by boolean</span></strong></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="14a2f36e-aed0-4789-abaa-6625ed8abf3e">true and false values</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>Example</strong></h3><p>Let's say that you want to extract the Legend identifying different <strong>Risk</strong> property values of Requirements in your model. To do this, complete the following steps:</p><ol><li>Open the Requirement Diagram in which you want to create the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values.</li><li>Right-click the shape of any Requirement and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Extract Legends </strong>OR in<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> the diagram toolbar, click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Extract Legends</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span></li><li>In the open dialog, select the <strong>Risk</strong> Legend, as shown in the figure below.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />The <em>Risk</em> Legend is automatically displayed and applied to the Requirements Diagram. The name of the element tag is used as the Legend name, and every possible <strong>Risk</strong> property value creates an individual Legend Item.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_legends_automatically.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends automatically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The process of creating the Legend identifying different Requirement risk values in a Requirement Diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9ab7ecd-4572-4c4e-bdff-c89a00845e76"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">579958103</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <em>Legends</em> sample model that comes with SysML plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Open the model from the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML directory</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1df00c6c-c046-4cf5-aed4-3c41e1ff9ea8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1287060641</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link><br /><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Legends" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating and managing Legend Items" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710652 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00320: Creating line jump

- page_id: `136710652`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710652/Creating+line+jump
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:50.463+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['line-jump', 'none-line-jump', 'horizontal-line-jump', 'vertical-line-jump']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Line jumps represent an intersection of lines. If you have a large diagram with lots of intersecting paths, line jumps make the diagram easier to understand. The line jumps are not displayed by default.

To add line jumps

1. Right-click the diagram pane and select Diagram Properties .
2. In the Diagram Properties dialog, select the Add Line Jumps To property value from the drop-down list:
  - None - specifies that the diagram use no line jumps (default value). [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_none.gif']
  - Horizontal Line - specifies that line jumps appear only on a horizontal line. [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_horizontal.gif']
  - Vertical Line**** - specifies that line jumps appear only on a vertical line. [ATTACHMENT filename='line_jumps_vertical.gif']

The line jump changes are applied for the current diagram only. To change line jumps for the whole project, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Line jumps represent an intersection of lines. If you have a large diagram with lots of intersecting paths, line jumps make the diagram easier to understand. The line jumps are not displayed by default.</p><p>To add line jumps</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram pane and select <strong>Diagram Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Add Line Jumps To</strong> property value from the drop-down list:<br /><ul><li><strong>None </strong>- specifies that the diagram use no line jumps (default value).<br /><ac:image ac:title="No line jumps" ac:alt="No line jumps"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_none.gif"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating line jump" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>Horizontal Line </strong>- specifies that line jumps appear only on a horizontal line.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Horizontal line jump" ac:alt="Horizontal line jump"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_horizontal.gif"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating line jump" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><strong>Vertical Line<strong> </strong></strong>- specifies that line jumps appear only on a vertical line.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Vertical line jump" ac:alt="Vertical line jump"><ri:attachment ri:filename="line_jumps_vertical.gif"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating line jump" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><br /><strong> </strong></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8304fe09-b127-4681-a347-cbead92601b8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The line jump changes are applied for the current diagram only. To change line jumps for the whole project, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714053 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00321: Creating merged column horizontally

- page_id: `136714053`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714053/Creating+merged+column+horizontally
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:04.564+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Tableprop tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050300468

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050300470

2050300467

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050300469

There are two methods that you can use to set column to merged column.

###### **$tableprop.mergeColumns(int number)**

When you put the statement in to a column, it will be set as merged column in a specified number.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns($Package.size())Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **$tableprop.mergeColumns(String stringNumber)**

When you put the statement in to a column, it will be set as merged column in a specified string of number.

For example:

**Template Code:**

| Project | $tableprop.mergeColumns(“3”)Packages |
| --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating merged column horizontally' space='MD2024x']*.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The number of cells will be increased by the value in mergeColumns.

For example:

**Template code #1:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns("5")Package |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #forcol($p in $Package)$p.name#endcol |  |  |

In case there are three packages, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Template code #2:**

| $tableprop.mergeColumns("5")Package |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

Output:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714058 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00322: Creating merged row vertically

- page_id: `136714058`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714058/Creating+merged+row+vertically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:05.046+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Tableprop tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050308208

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050308216

2050308206

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050308215

There are two methods that you can use to merge rows.

###### **$tableprop.mergeRows(Int number)**

When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified number.

For example:

**Template code**:

| #forrow($c in$sorter.sort($Class))$c.name$tableprop.mergeRows($c.ownedElement.size())#set($count=0)#foreach($attr in $c.ownedElement)#set($count = $count + 1) | $attr.name#if($count < $c.ownedElement.size()) |
| --- | --- |
| #end#if(false) | #end#end#endrow |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### **$tableprop.mergeRows(String stringNumber)**

When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified String of number. 
 For example:

**Template code**:

| #set($a = $array.createArray())#set($void = $a.add("A"))#set($void = $a.add("B"))#set($void = $a.add("C")) |  |
| --- | --- |
| #forrow($c in $a)Name$tableprop.mergeRows("3") | $c#endrow |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

mergeRows merges only existing cells in the same column and does not increase the number of rows or cells.

For example:

**Template code:**

| #forrow($package in $Package)$tableprop.mergeRows("5")Package | $package.name#endrow |
| --- | --- |

In case there are three packages, the output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbf39ba9-b922-440f-bee0-6aa8f6ecb6db"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308208</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff575217-07b0-46d1-b0f8-40d8d7febab0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="558da54e-d0cb-4023-a93d-989d21da6e3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308206</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e3e2992-fd17-4931-bab7-a8445a7c060c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050308215</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are two methods that you can use to merge rows.</p><h4><br /> <strong>$tableprop.mergeRows(Int number)</strong></h4><p>When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified number.</p><p>For example:</p><p><strong>Template code</strong>:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>#forrow($c in</p><p>$sorter.sort($Class))$c.name$tableprop.mergeRows($c.ownedElement.size())#set($count=0)</p><p>#foreach($attr in $c.ownedElement)#set($count = $count + 1)</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><br /></p><p>$attr.name#if($count &lt; $c.ownedElement.size())</p></div></td></tr><tr><td>#end#if(false)</td><td>#end#end#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="For Row Code As Merged Row.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating merged row vertically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$tableprop.mergeRows(String stringNumber)</strong></h4><p>When you put the statement into a row, it will be set as a merged row in a specified String of number.<br /> For example:</p><p><strong>Template code</strong>:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td colspan="2"><p>#set($a = $array.createArray())</p><p>#set($void = $a.add(&quot;A&quot;))</p><p>#set($void = $a.add(&quot;B&quot;))</p><p>#set($void = $a.add(&quot;C&quot;))</p></td></tr><tr><td>#forrow($c in $a)<strong>Name</strong>$tableprop.mergeRows(&quot;3&quot;)</td><td>$c#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="For Row Code As Merged Row 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating merged row vertically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c444ea47-0ae5-4fa4-abf5-284ee624bf8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>mergeRows merges only existing cells in the same column and does not increase the number of rows or cells.</p><p>For example:</p><p><strong>Template code:</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p style="text-align: center;">#forrow($package in $Package)</p><p style="text-align: center;">$tableprop.mergeRows(&quot;5&quot;)Package</p></td><td>$package.name#endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p>In case there are three packages, the output will be:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Template Code 1 (Merge Row - 3 Packages).png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating merged row vertically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709838 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00323: Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list

- page_id: `136709838`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709838/Creating+nested+parts+by+pasting+items+from+the+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:10.530+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Composite Structure diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1877349504

1877349516

1877349506

When you have an indented list of the items you want to create as nested parts in the diagram, you can copy the list and past it in the Composite Structure Diagram diagram pane.

You can copy the list from the text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, PowerPoint slide, etc.

To create parts by pasting items from the list

1. Copy the list.
2. Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.
3. In the open menu, select Elements .
4. Select the Part.
5. In the question dialog click Yes if you need to create nested parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The nested parts are created according to the copied list

1877349493

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x']

Composite Structure Diagram diagram pane.</p><p>You can copy the list from the text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, PowerPoint slide, etc.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create parts by pasting items from the list</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy the list.</li><li>Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.</li><li>In the open menu, select <strong>Elements</strong>.</li><li>Select the Part.</li><li>In the question dialog click <strong>Yes</strong> if you need to create nested parts.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_parts.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">The nested parts are created according to the copied list</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62806c11-b9ed-4f62-9aa4-2a079b61829b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1877349493</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715112 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00324: Creating new actions

- page_id: `136715112`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715112/Creating+new+actions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:21:10.715+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Adding new functionality
- labels: ['create-command', 'create-button', 'custom-command', 'custom-button']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

****You can find the code examples in

- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\actiontypes
- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\simpleconfigurators
- < modeling tool installation direc tory>\openapi\examples\selectionactions

##### Step #1: Create a new action class

All actions used in a modeling tool must be subclasses of the*com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MDAction* class (seejavadocfor more details). The following*MDAction*subclasses that are used for basic purposes are already created in a modeling tool:

- com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.actions.DefaultBrowserAction – an action class, used for a browser action. It enables to access some browser tree and nodes and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected browser nodes.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.actions.DefaultDiagramAction – an action class for a diagram action. It enables to access some diagram elements and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected diagram elements.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.PropertyAction – an action for changing some element or application property. It can be used for changing properties defined by a user.

You must override at least the *actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)* method and implement in it what this actions is going to do.

###### **Example #1****: A simple action**

```java

```

###### **Example #2****: An action for a browser**

```java

```

##### Step #2: Specify action properties

| Property | Function |
| --- | --- |
| ID | ID is a unique String for identifying an action. If the action ID is set to null, a new ID is generated. An action name can be used as ID. All MagicDraw (or another modeling tool developed by us) default actions IDs are defined in the com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID class (for more details, see JavaDoc). You can use these constants for accessing actions. Do not use it as new actions ID. |
| Name | The action name is visible in all GUI elements. |
| Shortcut and mnemonic | Every action can have an assigned keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts can be customized from the Environment Options dialog box.(see MagicDraw User Manual, Section “Environment Options”.)The action must have an ID assigned (not null). In other case, shortcuts cannot be restored after restarting an application. |
| Icon | Every action can have a small and large icon. A small icon is described as javax.swing.Action.SMALL_ICON and can be used in menu items. A large icon is used in toolbar buttons. The action for a toolbar must have a large icon, otherwise it is displayed as a button with an action name. java |
| Description | The action’s description that is shown as a tool tip text. |

****

##### Step #3: Describe Enabling/Disabling logic

There are two ways for controlling the update of the actions state:

1. Add an action to the predefined actions group.

Actions can be added into one of predefined actions groups. All actions of the one group are disabled/enabled together. Conditions for groups enabling/disabling and status updating are predefined and cannot be changed.

Example:

```java

```

2. **Implement the*****updateState()*********method for the action**.

Here you may describe all conditions when an action must be enabled and disabled. Example of the *updateState()* method for the browser shortcut menu action:

```java

```

If the action is not added to any group, the *updateState()*****method for all such actions will be invoked after executing any command and after closing/opening a project or window.

When some actions need to refresh their state, all actions without a group can be updated manually:

```java

```

##### Step #4: Configure actions

Every action must be added into an *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory* class. *ActionsCategory* is a small group for actions. It can be represented as a separator or submenu (a nested category).

Categories are added into the *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager* class, which is some kind of an actions container. One *ActionsManager* represents one GUI element – a menu bar, a shortcut menu, or a toolbar.

The following table explains how classes of a modeling tool maps into GUI elements:

|  | ActionsManager | Category | Action |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Menu | Menu bar | Menu | Menu item |
| Toolbar | All toolbars | One toolbar | Button |
| Shortcut Menu | Shortcut menu | Submenu | Menu item |

Actions in *ActionsManagers* are configured by many *Configurators*. A *Configurator* is responsible for adding or removing the action into a strictly defined place and position between other actions.

There are three types of configurators:

- com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator - a configurator for a general purpose. It is used for menus, toolbars, browser, and diagrams shortcuts.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.BrowserContextAMConfigurator - a configurator for configuring managers for a browser shortcut (pop-up) menu. It can access a browser tree and nodes.
- com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.DiagramContextAMConfigurator - a configurator for configuring shortcut menus in a diagram. It can access a diagram, selected diagram elements, and an element that requests a shortcut menu.

Once *ActionsManagers* for the main menu and all toolbars are created and configured, the actions can be only disabled but not removed later.

Shortcut menus are created on every invoking, so *ActionsManagers* are created and configured every time and actions can be added or removed every time.

###### **Exampl****e #1: Add some action into a browser’s shortcut menu**

```java

```

###### **Exampl****e #2: add some action into main menu, after creating a new project**

```java

```

##### Step #5: Register Configurator

All configurators are registered in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager* class. *ActionsConfiguratorsManager* enables to add or remove many configurators to every configuration predefined by a modeling tool.

All available configurations are accessible in the following way:

```java
Configurator(configurator);]]>
```

###### **Example: Add new configurator for CONTAINMENT_BROWSER_CONTEXT configuration**

```java

```

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\actiontypes</em></li><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\simpleconfigurators<br /></em></li><li>&lt;<em>modeling tool</em> <em>installation direc</em><em>tory&gt;\openapi\examples\selectionactions</em><em><br /></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #1: Create a new action class</h3><p><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">All actions used in a modeling tool must be subclasses of the </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MDAction</em> class (see </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">javadoc</span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> for more details). The following </span><em>MDAction</em><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> </span><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">subclasses that are used for basic purposes are already created in a modeling tool:</span></p><div class="WordSection1"><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.actions.DefaultBrowserAction</em> – an action class, used for a browser action. It enables to access some browser tree and nodes and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected browser nodes.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.actions.DefaultDiagramAction</em> – an action class for a diagram action. It enables to access some diagram elements and is recommended to use for performing some actions with the selected diagram elements.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.PropertyAction</em> – an action for changing some element or application property. It can be used for changing properties defined by a user.</li></ul><p>You must override at least the <em>actionPerformed(java.awt.event.ActionEvent)</em> method and implement in it what this actions is going to do.</p><h4><strong>    Example #1</strong><strong>: A simple action</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e84c3c8f-3f54-499e-bfae-e88922872360"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    class SimpleAction extends MDAction
    { 
        public SimpleAction(String id, String name)
        {
             super(id, name, null, null); 
        }
        /**
        * Shows message.
        */
        public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e)
        { 
            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(), "This is:" + getName()); 
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection2"><h4><strong>    Example #2</strong><strong>: An action for a browser</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ebd762e-1a8d-44d2-87a1-0f4cbe4aa4a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    public class BrowserAction extends DefaultBrowserAction
    {
        /**
        * Creates action with name "ExampleAction"
        */
        public BrowserAction()
        { 
          super("", "ExampleAction", null, null); 
        }
        public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e)
        {
            Tree tree = getTree();
            String text="Selected elements:";
            for (int i = 0; i < tree.getSelectedNodes().length; i++)
            { 
                Node node = tree.getSelectedNodes()[i]; 
                Object userObject = node.getUserObject(); 
                if (userObject instanceof BaseElement)
                {
                    BaseElement element = (BaseElement) userObject;
                    text += "\n"+element.getHumanName(); 
                }
            }
            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(MDDialogParentProvider.getProvider().getDialogParent(), text);
        }
    }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #2: Specify action properties</h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>ID</strong></td><td><p>ID is a unique String for identifying an action. If the action ID is set to <em>null, a </em>new ID is generated. An action name can be used as ID. All MagicDraw (or another modeling tool developed by us) default actions IDs are defined in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsID</em> class (for more details, see <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">JavaDoc</span>). You can use these constants for accessing actions. Do not use it as new actions ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>The action name is visible in all GUI elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Shortcut and mnemonic</strong></td><td><p>Every action can have an assigned keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts can be customized from the<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Environment Options </strong>dialog box.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="scroll-only" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d87b1ccd-736c-405f-9134-fe8e415c923b"><ac:rich-text-body>(see MagicDraw User Manual, Section “Environment Options”.)</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54fcf749-a24f-4355-9268-69fdc52cfa4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The action must have an ID assigned (not null). In other case, shortcuts cannot be restored after restarting an application.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Icon</strong></td><td><p>Every action can have a small and large icon. A small icon is described as <em>javax.swing.Action.SMALL_ICON</em> and can be used in menu items. A large icon is used in toolbar buttons. The action for a toolbar must have a large icon, otherwise it is displayed as a button with an action name.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exampleAction_button_with_name.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exampleAction_button.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2a386e7-c748-41d6-8a28-2ec2ae67f5c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[// setting an icon. On the toolbar, the button with an icon looks better than with the text.
action.setLargeIcon( new ImageIcon(getClass().getResource("main_toolbar_icon.gif") ) );]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Description</strong></td><td>The action’s description that is shown as a tool tip text.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">         </strong></p><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Step #3: Describe Enabling/Disabling logic</h3><p> There are two ways for controlling the update of the actions state:</p><ol><li><strong> Add an action to the predefined actions group.</strong></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Actions can be added into one of predefined actions groups. All actions of the one group are disabled/enabled together. Conditions for groups enabling/disabling and status updating are predefined and cannot be changed.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2c5c9b7-cfc9-44a7-844d-a298d4bed16a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[MDAction action = new MDAction("Example","Example",KeyEvent.VK_E, ActionsGroups.PROJECT_OPENED_RELATED);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>    2.  <strong>Implement the </strong><em><strong>updateState()</strong></em><strong> </strong><strong>method for the action</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Here you may describe all conditions when an action must be enabled and disabled. Example of the <em>updateState()</em> method for the browser shortcut menu action:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b09cb2a1-c539-47ae-bce8-d397356a100b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[        public void updateState()
        { 
           //action will be enabled only if there are some selected nodes. 
			Tree tree = getTree();
			if(tree != null)
			{
   				setEnabled(tree.getSelectedNode() != null);
			}
        }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>If the action is not added to any group, the <em>updateState()</em><strong> </strong>method for all such actions will be invoked after executing any command and after closing/opening a project or window.</p><p>When some actions need to refresh their state, all actions without a group can be updated manually:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96206eaa-b210-4a79-8dbf-476532529d1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ActionsStateUpdater.updateActionsState();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br style="margin-left: 30.0px;" /><h3>Step #4: Configure actions</h3><p>Every action must be added into an <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory</em> class. <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ActionsCategory</em> is a small group for actions. It can be represented as a separator or submenu (a nested category).</p></div><p>Categories are added into the <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager</em> class, which is some kind of an actions container. One <em>ActionsManager</em> represents one GUI element – a menu bar, a shortcut menu, or a toolbar. </p><div class="WordSection2">The following table explains how classes of a modeling tool maps into GUI elements:</div><div class="WordSection2"><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th style="text-align: left;">ActionsManager</th><th style="text-align: left;">Category</th><th style="text-align: left;">Action</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Menu</strong></td><td><span>Menu bar</span></td><td><span>Menu</span></td><td><span>Menu item</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>T</strong><strong>oolbar</strong></td><td><span>All toolbars</span></td><td><span>One toolbar</span></td><td><span>Button</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Shortcut Menu</strong></td><td><span>Shortcut menu</span></td><td><span>Submenu</span></td><td><span>Menu item</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>Actions in <em>ActionsManagers</em> are configured by many <em>Configurators</em>. A <em>Configurator</em> is responsible for adding or removing the action into a strictly defined place and position between other actions.</p></div><p>There are three types of configurators:</p><ul><li><em>com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for a general purpose. It is used for menus, toolbars, browser, and diagrams shortcuts.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.BrowserContextAMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for configuring managers for a browser shortcut (pop-up) menu. It can access a browser tree and nodes.</li><li><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.DiagramContextAMConfigurator</em> - a configurator for configuring shortcut menus in a diagram. It can access a diagram, selected diagram elements, and an element that requests a shortcut menu.</li></ul><p>Once <em>ActionsManagers</em> for the main menu and all toolbars are created and configured, the actions can be only disabled but not removed later.</p><p>Shortcut menus are created on every invoking, so <em>ActionsManagers</em> are created and configured every time and actions can be added or removed every time.</p><p><br /></p><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">    Exampl</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">e #1: Add some action into a browser’s shortcut menu</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02415b63-5905-407e-aa6e-fb1a2128fdc8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    final DefaultBrowserAction browserAction = ...
    BrowserContextAMConfigurator brCfg = new BrowserContextAMConfigurator()
    {
        // Implement configuration.
        // Add or remove some actions in ActionsManager.
        // A tree is passed as an argument, provides ability to access nodes.
       public void configure(ActionsManager mngr, Tree browser)
       {
           	// Actions must be added into some category.
           	// So create the new one, or add an action into the existing category.
           	MDActionsCategory category = new MDActionsCategory("", "");
          	category.addAction(browserAction);
            // Add a category into the manager.
          	// A category isn't displayed in a shortcut menu.
          	mngr.addCategory(category);
       }
       public int getPriority()
       {
          return AMConfigurator.MEDIUM_PRIORITY;
       }
   };]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><div class="WordSection5"><h4><strong>    Exampl</strong><strong>e #2: add some action into main menu, after creating a new project</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f42d1b97-1134-4495-bb00-36125c230ed4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    // Create an action.
    final MDAction someAction = ...
    AMConfigurator conf = new AMConfigurator()
    {
        public void configure(ActionsManager mngr)
        {
           // Searching for an action after which an insertion should be done. 
           NMAction found= mngr.getActionFor(ActionsID.NEW_PROJECT);
 
           // The action found, inserting 
           if( found != null )
           {
              	// find the category of the "New Project" action. 
              	ActionsCategory category = (ActionsCategory)mngr.getActionParent(found);


             	// Get all actions from this category (menu). 
             	List actionsInCategory = category.getActions();


             	//Add the action after the "New Project" action.
             	int indexOfFound = actionsInCategory.indexOf(found);
             	actionsInCategory.add(indexOfFound+1, someAction);
 
             	// Set all actions. 
             	category.setActions(actionsInCategory);
       		}
    	}

    	public int getPriority()
    	{
        	return AMConfigurator.MEDIUM_PRIORITY;
    	}
   	};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Step #5: Register Configurator</h3><p>All configurators are registered in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> class. <em>ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> enables to add or remove many configurators to every configuration predefined by a modeling tool.</p><p>All available configurations are accessible in the following way:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91f767b2-ebe7-457b-a37d-dba86f541a99"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().add<configuration_name>Configurator(configurator);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h4>    <strong>Example: Add new configurator for CONTAINMENT_BROWSER_CONTEXT configuration</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dffdfcae-5c92-4e62-bec1-7fb553cdd076"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    //See previous examples, how to create a configurator for browser actions.
    //Add the configurator into ActionsConfiguratorsManager.
    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addContainmentBrowserContextConfigurator(brCfg);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712898 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00325: Creating new Constraint element

- page_id: `136712898`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712898/Creating+new+Constraint+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:05.178+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Examples of Constraint types

INLINE

Before creating a new Constraint element, you must decide the Constraint type to create. The Constraint type defines a target for which the validation rule will be evaluated.

There are three types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint' space='MD2024x'] that can be evaluated:

- Classifier level Constraints . Constraints placed on the classes, datatypes and other [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space='MD2024x'] of the model are evaluated on all the instances of these classifiers (i.e. those [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024x'] that have the particular classifier set as their type). Constraints defined on some particular classifiers are evaluated on the instances of these particular classifiers when validating. Inheritance is taken into account; instances of the subclasses of the class are also validated.
- **Constraints on Metaclasses**. When a Constraint is placed on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MD2024x'] (one of the classes in the UML Standard Profile > UML2 Metamodel), this Constraint is evaluated on all the model elements of that kind (e.g. if the Constraint is placed on the Actor metaclass, then this Constraint applies to all actor elements in the model). The following is an example of a rule (specified in OCL2.0) mandating that all actor names in the model must be capitalized. These Constraints are useful for specifying generic rules that must apply on all the model elements of a particular kind. text
- Constraints on Stereotypes . When a Constraint is placed on some [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'] of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile' space='MD2024x'] , that Constraint applies to all the model elements that have these Stereotypes applied to them. These Constraints are useful when creating domain specific profiles. When adapting UML to some specific modeling domain, a Profile is usually created with extensions for that domain - Stereotypes, tags etc. The Constraints on these Stereotypes allow enforcing the rules of that domain.

#### NOTE: Recommendation

Recommendation

It is strongly recommend not to mix the Constraints from different metalevels into one validation suite. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='MD2024x']>]]>

#### TIP: Examples of Constraint types

Examples of Constraint types

The examples of each Constraint type can be found in the **model validation** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='model validation.mdzip'].*
- Find in your modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\model validation.mdzip.

You can create a new Constraint for :

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space='MD2024x'] , which is constrained – classes, datatypes, etc. (for classifier level Constraints). >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'] (for meta-classifier level Constraints). >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space='MD2024x'] located in a read-only profile. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='MD2024x']

classifierTo create Constraint for a classifier or Stereotype

1. Open the Specification window of a classifier or Stereotype. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']>]]>
2. On the left side of the window, select the Constraints property group.
3. On the right side of the window, click the Create button and select Constraint .
4. Type its name. The new Constraint is created in the model.

metaclassTo create Constraint for a metaclass

1. Select a package where you want to store the rule.
2. Right-click this package, select Create Element > Constraint .
3. Type its name. 
The new Constraint is created in the model. If you create a new Constraint for a metaclass, you must specify the **Constrained Element** property in the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Constraint Element property' space='MD2024x']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Constraint type defines a target for which the validation rule will be evaluated.</p><p>There are three types of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraints]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that can be evaluated:</p><ul><li><strong>Classifier level Constraints</strong>.
