<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715152 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00334: Creating new relationship objects

- page_id: `136715152`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715152/Creating+new+relationship+objects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:49:47.340+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A model element is a relationship, if it implements one of the following interfaces:

- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Relationship,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdbasicactivities.ActivityEdge ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.statemachines.mdbehaviorstatemachines.Transition ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.activities.mdextrastructuredactivities.ExceptionHandler ,
- com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.compositestructures.mdinternalstructures.Connector .

For checking if the model element is a relationship, call the *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.jmi.helpers.CoreHelper.isRelationship(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#isRelationship-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)* method.

For getting supplier and client elements of a relationship, use *CoreHelper**class getSupplierElement(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#getSupplierElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*, *getClientElement(Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#getClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*methods.

Use *CoreHelper**class**setSupplierElement(**Element, Element)* , *setClientElement(Element[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)*methods to set supplier and client elements of a relationship.

To create a new Relationship

1. Create a new relationship model element.
2. Set client and supplier model elements by using *CoreHelper* class *setSupplierElement(**Element, Element)* , *setClientElement(Element[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)Element[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/jmi/helpers/CoreHelper.html#setClientElement-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-)* methods.
3. Add a new relationship into some parent by using the com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element.setOwner( Element) method.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking element editing permissions' space='']

## PAGE 00335: Creating new validation rules

- page_id: `136712896`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712896/Creating+new+validation+rules

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Managing new validation rule

927074355

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Managing new validation rule

927074358

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Managing new validation rule

927074357

#### EXCERPT: Managing new validation rule

INLINE

You can create your own validation rules in the model and use them to check your model or keep them only for documentation purpose. You can locate new validation rules anywhere in your model, but usually they are created for the classifier, stereotype, or metaclass.

To create a new validation rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='MD2024x']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying validationRule stereotype' space='MD2024x']Apply «validationRule» stereotype.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Constraint Element property' space='MD2024x']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='MD2024x']
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining Constraint condition' space='MD2024x']Define Constraint expression. The new validation rule is created in the model .

#### NOTE: Managing new validation rule

Managing new validation rule

You can include a new validation rule to your own created validation suite.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='MD2024x']>]]>
- How to include the validation rules to validation suite Package >>

You can manage new validation rules in your model as any other elements.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024x']>]]>

927074354

**Related pages**

class="confluence-link">validation rule is created in the model</span>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Creating+validation+suites#Creatingvalidationsuites-fill">How to include the validation rules to validation suite Package &gt;&gt;</a></li></ul><p>You can manage new validation rules in your model as any other elements.</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with model elements" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715003 space=MD2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00336: Creating Numbering Customizations

- page_id: `136715003`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715003/Creating+Numbering+Customizations
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2024-05-29T14:33:29.602+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1099032786

INLINE

1099032788

INLINE

1099082233

INLINE

**On this page**

3

1099032787

INLINE

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic numbering mechanism' space=''] consists of the Numbering Customization and Numbering Scheme, shown in the figure below.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The structure of the Generic Numbering Mechanism.

Create numbering schemes (formats) and apply them to DSL elements in the modeling tool or plugin ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Business Modeler Plugin Documentation' space='CBMP2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation' space='UAF12P2024x'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation' space='UPDM2P2024x']).

You can create one or more numbering schemes for the same DSL element. You can also apply the same numbering scheme to several DSL elements. In this case, instances of different DSL elements will be numbered in sequence. For example, if you use the same numbering customization for both actors and use cases, all actors and use cases in a use case diagram will be numbered in sequence. Another example of numbering different DSL elements in sequence is shown in the figure below. The start events, end events, tasks, and gateways in the BPMN Process diagram are numbered using the same numbering scheme.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Numbering instances of different BPMN elements in a sequence

Find the instructions to customize the generic numbering mechanism to create your own numbering scheme in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your first numbering customization' space='']. Create your own numbering customization defining specific numbering properties using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space=''].

##### Basic concepts

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Numbering scheme | Defines a numbering style as well as number parts used to compose a DSL element number. A numbering schema can have one or more number parts. A numbering scheme is represented as a class with the «NumberingScheme» stereotype applied. |
| Number part | Represents a rule for calculating an individual part of the whole element number, as the element number is composed of one or more individual number parts. It can be a number, character, separator, or other. Number part is represented as a numbering scheme property with the «NumberPart» stereotype applied. |
| Numbering property | Indicates a DSL element property to store the element number and defines a numbering scheme used for the DSL element numbering. The numbering property is represented as a customization element property with the «AutoNumber» stereotype applied. |

##### Basic steps for creating numbering customization

To create a numbering customization for an element:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a diagram' space=''] .
2. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''] to customize a desired element.
3. Add a new attribute to the stereotype. This attribute will store the element number.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your first numbering customization' space=''].
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space=''] for the previously created stereotype.
6. Add a numbering attribute to the customization element. Reopen the project to apply changes.

##### Specifying custom numbering properties

To define your own specific numbering customization, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space='']. For general numbering it is a binary script. The following script example illustrates the multi-level numbering:

| binary;Binary;com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.LinkedDUCBinary; |
| --- |

Where *com.nomagic.magicdraw.autoid.LinkedDUCBinary* is your binary class that implements *INumberingAction.*

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom elements numbering' space=''].

1099032785

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your First Customization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='']

## PAGE 00337: Creating path elements

- page_id: `136715214`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715214/Creating+path+elements
- version_number: 2

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement* for given *ModelLink* between given client and supplier elements, use method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.createPathElement(**Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)*:

```java

```

The diagram is not passed into *createPathElement(**Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)*method. A new path element is created in the same diagram as client or supplier presentation elements.

****You can find the code examples in

- *<installation_directory>*\openapi\examples\symbolcreation
- **<installation_directory>*\openapi\examples\sequencecreation*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.paths.PathElement</em> for given <em>ModelLink</em> between given client and supplier elements, use method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.createPathElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement)</em>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18114fb6-b146-4506-ab53-7645667de387"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mddependencies.Dependency link = ...; 
    PresentationElement clientPE = ...;
    PresentationElement supplierPE = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test"); 
    PathElement path = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().createPathElement(link, clientPE, supplierPE);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The diagram is not passed into <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">createPathElement(</em><em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Element, PresentationElement, PresentationElement) </em>method. A new path element is created in the same diagram as client or supplier presentation elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em>\openapi\examples\symbolcreation</em></li><li><em><em><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em>\openapi\examples\sequencecreation</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fab8a4f-e60b-4600-ac77-cdd7ef0ea882"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709332 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00338: Creating path variables

- page_id: `136709332`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709332/Creating+path+variables
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-01-30T09:11:05.508+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004058347

1004058350

1004058348

INLINE

The main purpose of the path variables feature is to give additional sharing flexibility. It enables you to store paths in your project as named variables that can be easily adjusted on different machines.

Path Variables may be used in:

- Code engineering classpath
- Working directories
- Hyperlinks
- Modules paths
- Reports

To create a path variable

1. In the main menu, go to Options > Environment . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog opens.
2. On the left part of the dialog, select Path Variables .
3. In the Path Variables area click the Add button. The Path Variable dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_options_dialog.png']
4. In the appropriate boxes enter the path variable name and description.
5. Next to the Value box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button_long.png'] , navigate to the desired location, and click Open .
6. In the Path Variable dialog, click OK . Your custom path variable is created and shown in the Defined Path Variables list.
7. In the Environment Options dialog, click OK .

Every user can specify different values of the same path variables. However, path variables should be resolved only on demand, when they are used. If a modeling tool determines you can use path variables, once you specify your absolute path, the application automatically opens the **Use Path Variables** dialog box. For example, this dialog may open when in your project, you try to use another project **From a predefined location** and add a new path to the **Paths to used projects** list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not want your modeling tool to automatically display the **Use Path Variables** dialog box, clear the **Show suggestion to use path variables** check box. To use the original path and cancel this dialog, click the **Use Original** button.

To use a suggested path variable

- In the **Available forms** list, select a path and click the **Use Selected** button.

If you have your own documentation running on the local server, you can define the path in the *documentation.server* option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It enables you to store paths in your project as named variables that can be easily adjusted on different machines.</p><p>Path Variables may be used in:</p><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Code engineering classpath</span></li><li class="p"><p>Working directories</p></li><li class="p"><p>Hyperlinks</p></li><li class="p"><p>Modules paths</p></li><li class="p"><p>Reports</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a path variable</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>On the left part of the dialog, select <strong>Path Variables</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Path Variables</strong> area click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The <strong>Path Variable</strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the appropriate boxes enter the path variable name and description.</li><li>Next to the <strong>Value</strong> box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, navigate to the desired location, and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Path Variable</strong> dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. Your custom path variable is created and shown in the <strong>Defined Path Variables</strong> list.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> </li></ol><p>Every user can specify different values of the same path variables. However, path variables should be resolved only on demand, when they are used. If a modeling tool determines you can use path variables, once you specify your absolute path, the application automatically opens the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Use Path Variables</strong> dialog box. For example, this dialog may open when in your project, you try to use another project <strong>From a predefined location</strong> and add a new path to the <strong>Paths to used projects</strong> list.<br /> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_path_variables_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br />If you do not want your modeling tool to automatically display the <span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><strong>Use Path Variables</strong></span> dialog box, clear the <span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><strong>Show suggestion to use path variables</strong></span> check box. To use the original path and cancel this dialog, click the <span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><strong>Use Original</strong> button.</span></p><p><span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><br /></span></p><p><span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;">To use a suggested path variable</span></p><hr /><ul><li>In the <span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><strong>Available forms</strong></span> list, select a path and click the <span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><strong>Use Selected</strong> button.</span></li></ul><p><span class="hcp1" style="line-height: 1.42857;"><br /></span></p><p>If you have your own documentation running on the local server, you can define the path in the <em>documentation.server</em> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715287 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00339: Creating PopupMenu or other menu

- page_id: `136715287`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715287/Creating+PopupMenu+or+other+menu
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:32:16.773+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Using and extending other UI components
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

API provides a way to create a popup menu from actions. Of course, you can use the standard java api for that, but we provide our own creators if you use our Actions.

Use*com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MenuCreatorFactory.getMenuCreator()* to get an instance of a creator.

Use *com.nomagic.actions.ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(**ActionsManager)* to create a popup menu.

Steps to create a popup menu would be

1. Create an instance of com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager.
2. Add com.nomagic.actions.NMAction actions and com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory categories into ActionsManager .
3. Call ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu( ActionsManager) to create a popup for your actions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new actions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Of course, you can use the standard java api for that, but we provide our own creators if you use our Actions.</p><p>Use<em> com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.MenuCreatorFactory.getMenuCreator()</em> to get an instance of a creator.</p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(</em><em>ActionsManager)</em> to create a popup menu.</p><p><br /></p><p>Steps to create a popup menu would be</p><ol><li>Create an instance of <em>com.nomagic.actions.ActionsManager.</em></li><li>Add<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.actions.NMAction</em> actions and<em> com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory</em> categories into <em>ActionsManager</em>.</li><li>Call <em>ActionsMenuCreator.createPopupMenu(</em><em>ActionsManager)</em>  to create a popup for your actions.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bac02bc-b67f-4173-a430-6fd772fe6374"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714831 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00340: Creating Profiles

- page_id: `136714831`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714831/Creating+Profiles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:51.101+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Profiles can be created in any project. However, often many projects use the same profiles. In order to reuse the same profile, it must be created as an independent file with shared data called a module.

To create a Profile as a module

1. Create a new project.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'], right-click the root **Model** and create a new Profile.
3. Create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MD2024x'] inside the Profile.
4. Create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']in the diagram.
5. Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2024x'] between them if needed.
6. In the Model Browser, from the created Profile shortcut menu, choose Project Usages > Share Packages .
7. Select the Profile as shared, enter its description, and click OK .
8. Save the project.

Now you may use this profile in other projects.

To use the created profile in other projects

- From the **File** menu, choose **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD190']** and select the project file. Only shared packages of the module will be loaded into the project.

#### NOTE: Tip

Tip

If you don’t want to see particular information, move elements from shared packages into the root model or other non-shared packages in your module.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project partitioning' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709673 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00342: Creating projects for Ecore modeling

- page_id: `136709673`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709673/Creating+projects+for+Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:03.774+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305881821

305881823

305881822

You can use the following ways to create a project for Ecore modeling: 
 
To create a new project from the Ecore template

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **New Project**. The **New Project** dialog opens.
2. In the **Other** domain, select **Project from Template**.
3. Type a project name.
4. Specify the project location.
5. Under **Select template**, expand **Metamodeling** and then select **Ecore Template**.
6. Click **OK** when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 
To apply the Ecore profile to a project

The Ecore profile can be applied only to existing UML or CMOF / EMOF projects.

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Use Project...** The **Use Project** wizard opens.
2. Under **Select a project to use**, click **From predefined location**. The **Paths to used projects** list appears.
3. Select <*install root>\profiles*. The content of the <*install root>\profiles* folder is listed bellow.
4. Select *Ecore_Profile.xml* in the list.
5. Click **Next**, if you want to change used projects usage settings.
6. Click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 If your Ecore model references some standard Ecore elements (such as standard data types (for example, EShort) or standard metaclasses (for example, EStructuralFeature), you need to use the standard Ecore library in your project. 
 
To use the standard Ecore library in a project

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Use Project...** The **Use Project** wizard opens.
2. Under **Select a project to use**, click **From predefined location**. The **Paths to used projects** list appears.
3. Select <*installroot>\modelLibraries*. The content of the <*install root>\modelLibraries* folder is listed bellow.
4. Select *Ecore.mdzip* in the list.
5. Click **Next**, if you want to change used projects usage settings.
6. Click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

305881820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new projects from templates' space='MD2024x']

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714994 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00343: Creating Property Groups and Subgroups

- page_id: `136714994`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714994/Creating+Property+Groups+and+Subgroups
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:02:03.385+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Create your own groups and subgroups to display the properties of both standard UML metaclasses or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']. You can also choose where you want to see these property groups and subgroups. Property groups and subgroups can be visible in the following places:

- Element’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property groups and subgroups in the element's SpecificationWindow

- Element’s Properties panel (at the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] ).
- Element’s shortcut menu > Go To > submenu.

###### Property groups and subgroups in the element's shortcut menu

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying or hiding elements in compartments on shapes' space='']**Compartment Edit** dialog .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria, layout, and depth' space='']**Criterion Editor** dialog for editing the relation’s criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space=''].

To create a property group

1. In the Model Browser, right-click a customization element and choose Create Element > Property Group .
2. Type the attribute’s name (used to name the property group).
3. Open the attribute’s Specification window.
4. Edit the property values in the general specification pane. Each property is described in the description area in the Specification window.
5. Click Close .

A stereotype [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='«propertyGroup» Properties' space=''] has been automatically applied to the newly created attribute.

To create a property subgroup

1. Choose the customization element you want to create a property subgroup for. The customization element must have at least one attribute with the stereotype «propertyGroup» applied.
2. Right-click the attribute with a stereotype «propertyGroup» and choose Create Element > Property Group .
3. Type the attribute’s name (used to name the property subgroup).
4. Open the attribute’s Specification window.
5. In the general specification pane, edit the property values (properties are described in the table below). A subgroup does not inherit the properties of the group. You must specify them for each subgroup separately.
6. Click Close .

A stereotype «propertyGroup» has been automatically applied to the newly created attribute.

#### INFO: Note

Note

The stereotype «propertyGroup» is applied when creating either a property group or a subgroup.

Depending on the attribute hierarchy, the attribute will either be a property group or subgroup:

1. If an attribute with a stereotype «propertyGroup» is owned by a customization element, it is a property group.
2. If an attribute with a stereotype «propertyGroup» is owned by another attribute with the same stereotype, then it is a property subgroup.

#### INFO: «propertyGroup» properties

«propertyGroup» properties

All properties of a stereotype «propertyGroup» are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='«propertyGroup» Properties' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<strong>Property Group</strong>.</li><li>Type the attribute’s name (used to name the property group).</li><li>Open the attribute’s Specification window.</li><li>Edit the property values in the general specification pane. Each property is described in the description area in the Specification window.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><div class="column"><p><span> <br /></span></p><p><span>A stereotype <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="926924d3-3697-43da-b310-6788d29c86c9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="«propertyGroup» Properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[«propertyGroup»]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></ac:inline-comment-marker> has been automatically applied to the newly created attribute. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a property subgroup</p><hr /><ol><li>Choose the customization element you want to create a property subgroup for. The customization element must have at least one attribute with the stereotype «propertyGroup» applied.</li><li>Right-click the attribute with a stereotype «propertyGroup» and choose <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Property Group</strong>.</li><li>Type the attribute’s name (used to name the property subgroup).</li><li>Open the attribute’s Specification window.</li><li>In the general specification pane, edit the property values (properties are described in the table below). A subgroup does not inherit the properties of the group. You must specify them for each subgroup separately.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p>A stereotype «propertyGroup» has been automatically applied to the newly created attribute.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b24be5e3-3718-4650-8fcc-645c3789f20b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The stereotype «propertyGroup» is applied when creating either a property group or a subgroup.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Depending on the attribute hierarchy, the attribute will either be a property group or subgroup:</p><ol><li>If an attribute with a stereotype «propertyGroup» is owned by a customization element, it is a property group.</li><li>If an attribute with a stereotype «propertyGroup» is owned by another attribute with the same stereotype, then it is a property subgroup. </li></ol></div><div class="column"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="groups_and_subgroups.png" /></ac:image></div><div class="column"><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4762b923-6321-41cf-88ad-1e2b26459bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">«propertyGroup» properties</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>All properties of a <span> stereotype «propertyGroup» are described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="«propertyGroup» Properties" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p></div></div></div></div></div></div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6cf0582-f701-4d85-8a56-2af3c971fe97"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="52b8e4c3-ef79-4f70-8df2-fdf042b6b0e6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709946 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00344: Creating relation map

- page_id: `136709946`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709946/Creating+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:17.154+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['create-relation-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a relation map

1. Select an element, for which you need to create the relation map. This element is the context of the relation map.
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N. Type “rel” and press Enter .
  - On the Analyze menu, click Create Relation Map .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Analysis Diagrams > Relation Map .
  - Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select Related Elements > Create Relation Map .

The newly created relation map opens on the right side of the application window.

- The context of the relation map automatically becomes the owner of it when using any of the last two approaches.
- When using other approaches, you can specify another element as the owner.
- The actions relevant for diagrams are valid for relation maps as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This element is the context of the relation map.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Diagrams</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. Type “rel” and press </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>On the main toolbar, click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> button. Type “rel” and press </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Press Ctrl+N. Type “rel” and press <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Enter</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>On the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Analyze</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> menu, click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt;</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Analysis Diagrams</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Right-click the element and from the shortcut menu select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Related Elements</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt;</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Relation Map</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p>The newly created relation map opens on the right side of the application window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="198500c2-d1fb-45cd-bbf8-9975f8f67040"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The context of the relation map automatically becomes the owner of it when using any of the last two approaches.</li><li>When using other approaches, you can specify another element as the owner.</li><li>The actions relevant for diagrams are valid for relation maps as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715095 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00345: Creating required files and folders structure

- page_id: `136715095`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715095/Creating+required+files+and+folders+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:05.228+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Distributing Resources
- labels: ['distributed-resources', 'distributing-diagrams', 'distributing-profiles', 'distributing-templates', 'distributing-samples', 'distributing-documentation', 'distributing-plugins']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

960869435

960869448

960869437

To distribute resources, you must create the required files and folders for a particular resource type. Some of the resource file names should match the standard names.

For each resource file, there should be a created folders structure, which should match the folders structure of the program installation folder.

To distribute resources, you must create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='MD2024x'].

##### Distributing**Custom** Diagrams

A required file for the custom diagram distribution is *descriptor.xml* - a Custom Diagram descriptor provided by MagicDraw Resource Manager. The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The custom diagram type (in diagram descriptor.xml file) should match the folder name it is placed in.

For example, the custom diagram with type *My Custom Structure Diagram* should be placed in a folder named **My Custom Structure Diagram**.

on about creating new diagram types,

UML Profiling and DSL UserGuide.pdf

##### Distributing Profiles

You can distribute your profile as the following structure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For more information about workingwithProfiles see *MagicDraw UserManual.pdf*and *UML Profiling and DSL UserGuide.pdf*.

##### Distributing Templates

Files for the template distribution are as follows:

- CustomTemplate.mdzip (required) - the template project file.
- CustomTemplate.html - the description of the template project.
- CustomTemplate.html - the description of the template group.

The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:**

*[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

****

##### Distributing Samples and Documentation

You can distribute your created samples and documentation and import them into your modeling tool with the MagicDraw ResourceManager. The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Distributing Plugins

Required files for a plugin distribution are as follows:

- plugins.xml - A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='MD2024x'] .
- customPlugin.jar - jarred [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin classes' space='MD2024x'] . You may select any title for this file.

The file and folder structure is depicted in the following figure:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The plugin term may include all resources that could be distributed, such as custom diagrams, profiles, templates, samples, and others.

960869434

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource manager descriptor file' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin descriptor' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugin classes' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713472 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00346: Creating revisions and branches

- page_id: `136713472`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713472/Creating+revisions+and+branches
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-12-12T14:21:24.299+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1437666276

1437666288

1437666275

**On this page**

4

1437666277

Project branching enables multiple users to work on the same model at the same time. To do this, you need to create a project branch which is a copy of the project. You can modify the branch as required, independently of other branches. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model merge in Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2021xR2'] back together when required. Conflicts between the branches are resolved during the merge process.

##### Understanding project branching

To understand how project branching works, you should be familiar with the following terms:

- Iteration - the smallest possible milestone of the project development. In other words, it is a project version you can open and review created after [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']committing the project to the **3D**EXPERIENCE****platform . Iterations are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 0 (zero).
- Revision - a larger project development milestone consisting of one or several iterations. Revisions are labeled by letters from A to Z. Note that iterations of different revisions within a branch are numbered independently as shown below.
- Branch - a copy of a project made at a certain point of the project development. Branches are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 1 (branch 1 is the root project). Each project branch can be developed independently and later merged with another branch. Every project branch consists of one or several revisions. Revisions of different project branches are labeled independently as shown below.

branch

revision.iteration

[3] B.4

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project branching in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

##### Creating revisions and branches

You can create new branches and revisions from any revision of any branch of a project. There is no limit to the number of branches and revisions you can create.

To create a project branch or revision

1. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Select the project you want to branch and click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_revision.png'] in the dialog toolbar.
  2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='select_revision_button.png'] next to the project you want to branch.
3. In the Select Revision dialog, click the Edit Branches button.
4. Expand project branches in the open dialog and select the revision from which you want to create another revision or branch. Revisions and branches can be created only from the latest iteration of the selected revision. The revision and branch creation buttons will be inactive if you select any other iteration of a revision.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. Click the Create Revision button to create a revision from the selected revision.
  2. Click the Create Branch button to create a branch from the selected revision. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_branches_and_revisions.png']

After completing the above steps, a revision or branch is created and displayed in the **Edit Branches** dialog. New revisions and branches are named automatically following the rules described in . In addition, a created revision or the first revision of a created branch holds information about the iteration it was created from. This information is displayed between brackets next to the name of a new revision.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41470364-539e-49c6-bc9a-eb865e9382de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1437666276</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08a4f73d-d2a9-41ba-9d74-e4e7fc5d05b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1437666288</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e369e93-acbb-4cc4-ab39-c1da97b39ffe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1437666275</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a238fa47-3645-45f9-a79b-da94ffb797cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3a5e94fd-3665-4418-9ff1-85cb56fd7e87"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1437666277</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Project branching enables multiple users to work on the same model at the same time. To do this, you need to create a project branch which is a copy of the project. You can modify the branch as required, independently of other branches. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2021xR2" ri:content-title="Model merge in Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Branches can be merged]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> back together when required. Conflicts between the branches are resolved during the merge process.</p><h3><br />Understanding project branching</h3><p>To understand how project branching works, you should be familiar with the following terms:</p><ul><li><strong>Iteration</strong> - the smallest possible milestone of the project development. In other words, it is a project version you can open and review created after <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>committing the project to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE<strong> </strong>platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>. Iterations are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 0 (zero).</li><li><strong>Revision</strong> -  a larger project development milestone consisting of one or several iterations. Revisions are labeled by letters from A to Z. Note that iterations of different revisions within a branch are numbered independently as shown below.</li><li><strong>Branch</strong> - a copy of a project made at a certain point of the project development. Branches are labeled by numbers in ascending order starting from 1 (branch 1 is the root project). Each project branch can be developed independently and later merged with another branch. Every project branch consists of one or several revisions. Revisions of different project branches are labeled independently as shown below.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f6b060c-8c84-49e7-9e59-26550176fab3"><ac:rich-text-body>Since iterations of different project branches are not unique, a combination of a branch, revision, and iteration is used to define a certain iteration. A unique project iteration is labeled in the following format: [<em>branch</em>] <em>revision.iteration</em>. For example iteration 4 of revision B of branch 3 would be labeled as <strong>[3] B.4</strong>. You can see the iteration of an open project in the header of the modeling tool window next to the project name.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="branching.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Project branching in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</h6><h3><br />Creating revisions and branches</h3><p>You can create new branches and revisions from any revision of any branch of a project. There is no limit to the number of branches and revisions you can create.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a project branch or revision</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the project you want to branch and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_revision.png" /></ac:image> in the dialog toolbar.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_revision_button.png" /></ac:image> next to the project you want to branch.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Select Revision</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Expand project branches in the open dialog and select the revision from which you want to create another revision or branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2470595-3aad-4ab0-816b-c05042e93f67"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Revisions and branches can be created only from the latest iteration of the selected revision. The revision and branch creation buttons will be inactive if you select any other iteration of a revision.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the <strong>Create Revision</strong> button to create a revision from the selected revision.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create Branch</strong> button to create a branch from the selected revision.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_branches_and_revisions.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, a revision or branch is created and displayed in the <strong>Edit Branches</strong> dialog. New revisions and branches are named automatically following the rules described in <ac:link ac:anchor="Understanding project branching" />. In addition, a created revision or the first revision of a created branch holds information about the iteration it was created from. This information is displayed between brackets next to the name of a new revision.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715335 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00347: Creating script

- page_id: `136715335`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715335/Creating+script
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:35:01.120+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Jython Scripting
- labels: ['plugin-descriptor', 'python', 'jython']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Let's describe creating a script in an example. We will create a script showing a message on a program startup. The creation process consists of three steps:

1. Creating a directory
2. Writing a script descriptor
3. Writing a script code

****

**Ste****p #1: Create Directory**

In the *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython*folder, create a *scripts*sub-folder, and then a folder for the particular script. For example, *plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython/scripts/example*

**Ste****p #2: Write Script Descriptor**

A script descriptor is a file written in XML and named *script.xml*. The script descriptor provides information about a script file to run, version of script, ID, and other.

In the created directory, create a *script.xml*file:

```xml
]]>
```

The following table describes the *script.xml* file structure:

| Element | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| script | Attributes |  |
| Name | Description |  |
| id | A scrip ID, should be unique. Used to identify a script. Example: “my.first.script.0” |  |
| name | A script name. No strict rules applied to this attribute. Example: “Example script” |  |
| version | A script version. Allows numbers separated with one dot value. Examples: “1.0”, “0.1” |  |
| provider-name | A script provider name. A company or an author name. Example: "No Magic" |  |
| script-file | A relative path to a script file. This file will be executed. Example:”main.py” |  |
| requires-api | A program API version required by a script. Example:”1.0” |  |

**Ste****p #3: Write Script Code**

Then in the same directory, create the *main.py*file:

```py

```

After saving files, restart your modeling tool. On a program startup, a message dialog should appear.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**V****ariable****s Passed to Script**

The program passes the one variable to the script *pluginDescriptor*. This variable contains information from the parsed *script.xml* file. A variable is an instance of a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython.PythonPluginDescriptor* class.

A script can retrieve the script directory and other necessary information from the *pluginDescriptor* variable. There is no need to change any other fields for this variable.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

JOptionPane.showMessageDialog( None, "I am a script!!!")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>After saving files, restart your modeling tool. This variable contains information from the parsed <em>script.xml</em> file. A variable is an instance of a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.jpython.PythonPluginDescriptor</em> class.</p><p>A script can retrieve the script directory and other necessary information from the <em>pluginDescriptor</em> variable. There is no need to change any other fields for this variable.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712412 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00348: Creating script operations

- page_id: `136712412`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712412/Creating+script+operations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:34.921+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

69158788

69158801

69158791

Use the script operation, when you need to specify an inline expression in OCL 2.0, as Java binary, or in any of MagicDraw supported script languages. MagicDraw supports any script language that has a JSR-223-compliant engine.

Adding a script operation directly into a structured expression is the same as defining an operation in the model and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Calling operations from the model' space='MD2024x'].

To create a script operation

1. Click Create operation on the left side of the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_operation_in_relation_criteria_dialog.png']
2. Click the **Script** icon on the right side of the dialog.
3. If needed, change the operation name.
4. Click the three dots button next to the **Style** option to define the style****for your script operation. The Style option is available only in the **Dependency Criteria** and**Relation Criteria**dialogs.
5. From the **Language** drop-down list, select a language. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended languages (Binary or Groovy). The icon color next to the language selection reflects performance, (i.e., green for fast, yellow for medium, and red for slow). If the performance is unknown, an info icon appears. If the engine of the language you need is not built into MagicDraw by default, you can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a scripting language to MagicDraw' space='MD2024x']to the application yourself. Make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled!
6. Type in the Body box. Use the syntax of the selected language.
7. In the **Listeners Configuration** box, type a reference to the java class, which specifies when the result of the script operation must be recalculated. The listeners configuration is not necessary, but using it can improve the performance. 
You can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram events' space='MD2024x']using MagicDraw API.
8. Click the three dots button next to the **Results Filter By Type** box and select one or more element types to filter the result of the script operation. 
The script operation is created. Now you can create a new one, where the result of the previously created operation is used as an argument.

69158787

**Related pages**

- Creating new operations
  - Calling operations from the model

Make sure the Automaton plugin is enabled!</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Type in the <strong>Body</strong> box. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712077 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00349: Creating Setters and Getters

- page_id: `136712077`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712077/Creating+Setters+and+Getters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:07.864+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

176217794

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

176217796

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

176217795

#### NOTE: Modeling Tools

Modeling Tools

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect and Enterprise editions only.

Setters and Getters are common operations that contain almost every [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x']. With the help of our modeling tool, **set** and **get** operations for Class attributes can be generated automatically.

To create a setter or a getter

There are two ways of creating a setter or a getter:

1. From the shortcut menu of the selected Class, select Tools > Code Engineering , and then select Create Setters/Getters .
  1. The Select Attributes/Association Ends dialog opens.
2. Add a tagged value "getter/setter for attribute=attribute_name" to the selected Class.

The names of created operations (**setters**) are combined according to the following format:

*public void set + <attribute name> (<attribute type> <attribute name>)*

#### INFO: Example

Example

If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated **setter** will look this way:

- public void setx (int x);

The names of created operations (**getters**) are combined according to the following format:

*public <attribute type> get + <attribute name> ( )*

#### INFO: Example

Example

If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated **getter** will look this way:

- public int getx ();

176217793

**Related Pages**

- Class
- Tools

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

With the help of our modeling tool, <strong>set</strong> and <strong>get</strong> operations for Class attributes can be generated automatically.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a setter or a getter</p><hr /><p>There are two ways of creating a setter or a getter:</p><ol><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Class, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Code Engineering</strong>, and then select <strong>Create Setters/Getters</strong>.<ol><li>The <strong>Select Attributes/Association Ends</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li>Add a tagged value &quot;getter/setter for attribute=attribute_name&quot; to the selected Class.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The names of created operations (<strong>setters</strong>) are combined according to the following format:</p><p><em>public void set + &lt;attribute name&gt; (&lt;attribute type&gt; &lt;attribute name&gt;) </em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f0e47a8-6abe-4cec-ba76-0d641e5a9784"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated <strong>setter</strong> will look this way: </span></p><ul><li><em>public void setx (int x);</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>The names of created operations (<strong>getters</strong>) are combined according to the following format: </span></p><p><em>public &lt;attribute type&gt; get + &lt;attribute name&gt; ( ) </em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb5c311-2a49-4218-93de-cc9dae32743d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you have an attribute called x of type int, then the generated <strong>getter</strong> will look this way: </span></p><ul><li><em>public int getx ();</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8f17678-7e4a-469b-b87b-6b579195beac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">176217793</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Class">Class</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Tools">Tools</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715213 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00350: Creating shape elements

- page_id: `136715213`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715213/Creating+shape+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:19:46.058+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement* for given *ModelElement* in the given***com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement*, use method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#createShapeElement(...)*. The location of the created shape will be (0,0).

The following code example shows how to do this:

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\symbolcreation
- <installation_directory*>\openapi\examples\sequencecreation*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>To create a <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.shapes.ShapeElement</em> for given <em>ModelElement</em> in the given<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement</em>, use method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager#createShapeElement(...)</em>. The location of the created shape will be (0,0).</p><p>The following code example shows how to do this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39605a8a-f11b-4266-98b9-f68effb6285e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class clazz = ...; 
    DiagramPresentationElement diagram = ...; 
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    ShapeElement shape = PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().createShapeElement(clazz, diagram); 
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\symbolcreation</em></li><li><em><span>&lt;installation_directory</span><em>&gt;\openapi\examples\sequencecreation</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b78620b-9e53-477a-b97f-fe624969d0a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711148 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00351: Creating Signal receptions for Messages

- page_id: `136711148`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711148/Creating+Signal+receptions+for+Messages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:14:31.845+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Message
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372415134

372415137

372415136

INLINE

Assigning a Signal reception to a Message is very similar to the procedure of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Operations to Messages' space=''].

These two conditions must be satisfied when creating a new Signal reception:

- At least one Signal must exist in your project.
- A possible Signal reception receiver (an activation to which the Message points) must have a type assigned.

To create a new signal reception for the message

1. Draw a send Message between the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space=''] or select an existing one on a diagram pane.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Signals to Messages' space=''] .
3. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Click the button in the shape of a small red circle at the end of the Message name. Define the Signal reception property values in the Signal reception [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. The default name of the Signal reception is the name of the Message for which the Signal reception is being created. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - From the Message shortcut menu, select the **Create New Signal Reception** (the command with the small red circle icon, as shown above). Define Signal reception property values in the Signal reception [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. The default name of the Signal reception is the name of the Message for which the Signal reception is being created.

372415133

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711278 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00352: Creating Slots on Link creation automatically

- page_id: `136711278`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711278/Creating+Slots+on+Link+creation+automatically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:30.447+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Slot
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Link' space='MD2024x'] between two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024x']s, slot creation for an Instance is suggested automatically.

1. On the diagram draw two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] shapes - A and B Classes.
2. Draw an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024x'] with roles between them.
3. Draw two Instance shapes. Define Class A as a classifier of the first Instance and Class B as a classifier of the second Instance.
4. Draw a Link between Instances. The Select Association dialog will open.
5. The association which is drawn between Classes will be automatically selected in the Select Association dialog. Click OK .
6. The Create Slots dialog will open. Select Association ends. Ends of Association which is drawn between class A and Class B will be selected automatically in the Create Slots dialog. Click **OK**. Slots for Instance will be created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Link" /></ac:link> between two <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link>s, slot creation for an Instance is suggested automatically. </span></p><ol><li class="kadov-p">On the diagram draw two <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> shapes - A and B Classes.</li><li class="kadov-p">Draw an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link> with roles between them.</li><li class="kadov-p">Draw two Instance shapes. Define Class A as a classifier of the first Instance and Class B as a classifier of the second Instance.</li><li class="kadov-p">Draw a Link between Instances. The <span class="hcp1">Select Association</span> dialog will open.</li><li class="kadov-p">The association which is drawn between Classes will be automatically selected in the <span class="hcp1">Select Association</span> dialog. Click <strong><span class="hcp1">OK</span></strong>.</li><li class="p"><p>The <span class="hcp1">Create Slots</span> dialog will open. Select Association ends. Ends of Association which is drawn between class A and Class B will be selected automatically in the <span class="hcp1">Create Slots</span> dialog. Click <strong><span class="hcp1">OK</span></strong>. Slots for Instance will be created.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711281 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00353: Creating smart packages

- page_id: `136711281`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711281/Creating+smart+packages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:30.753+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A smart package can be created in one of the following ways:

- From the shortcut menu of an element . The newly created smart package is empty.
- From the search results (by saving either the search options or the search results as a smart package). The newly created smart package has either dynamic or static contents.

To create a smart package from the shortcut menu of an element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the element. The shortcut menu opens. The element should be a namespace, such as a package, a class, or a requirement.
2. Select Create Element > [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_package_button.png'] Smart Package .
3. Type a name for the new smart package.
4. Press Enter to finish.

The new smart package is created, and you can proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space='MD2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart package properties' space='MD2024x'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a smart package from the search results

1. Open the **Find**dialog, define search options, and perform the search. The search results are displayed in the **Search Results**tab on the Model Browser. For more information about defining search options and analyzing the search results, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MD2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='MD2024x'].
2. On the toolbar of the Search Results tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_as_smart_package_button.png'] and select one of the following commands: [ATTACHMENT filename='save_query_as_smart_package.png'] The element Selection dialog opens.
  - Save Query as Smart Package to create a new smart package with dynamic contents. The search options will be saved as the Query property value.
  - Save Results as Smart Package to create a new smart package with static contents. The search results will be saved as values of the Additional Elements property.
3. In the dialog, select an owner of the new smart package and click OK .
4. In the Containment tree, type a name for the new smart package.
5. Press Enter to finish.

The new smart package is created, and you can proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space='MD2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart package properties' space='MD2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Profiles' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The search results are displayed in the <strong>Search Results </strong>tab on the Model Browser.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21ec36f3-be82-4abe-8e7a-fff7b110baca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about defining search options and analyzing the search results, refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Search Results tab" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the toolbar of the <strong>Search Results </strong>tab, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_as_smart_package_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating smart packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select one of the following commands:<br /><br /><ul><li><strong>Save Query as Smart Package </strong>to create a new smart package with dynamic contents. The search results will be saved as values of the <strong>Additional Elements</strong> property.</li></ul><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_query_as_smart_package.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating smart packages" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><p>The element Selection dialog opens.<br /><br /></p></li><li>In the dialog, select an owner of the new smart package and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>In the Containment tree, type a name for the new smart package.</li><li>Press Enter to finish.</li></ol></div><p>The new smart package is created, and you can proceed to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing contents of smart package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[managing its contents]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart package properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reviewing its specific properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1cb6614-da6a-4a7b-adf1-3ec18e11a18c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Profiles" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714841 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00354: Creating Structured Expression Tags

- page_id: `136714841`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714841/Creating+Structured+Expression+Tags
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:52.378+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tags

1534683666

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tags

1534683677

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tags

1534683667

If you need a tag value, as defined by using the dialog for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024x'], you must create a structured expression tag definition.

To create a structured expression tag definition

1. Create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Right-click the stereotype and select Create Element > Property to create a new tag definition.
3. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of the tag definition and select the Type property. Click the property specification cell and select the StructuredExpression value as a type. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_structured_expression_tag_definition.png']

Once the tag definition is created, you can apply the stereotype with this tag definition on an element in your model. Then, in the Specification window of the element, click the **Tags** property group, select the structured expression tag, and click the **Create Value** button to define the value.

#### NOTE: Tags

Tags

The structured expression tag can also be represented as a regular property in the Specification window and **Properties tab**of the element.You must then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024x'] for the stereotype with this tag definition.

1534683665

**Related Pages**

- Applying Stereotypes
- Stereotype
- Tag
- Creating Customization Data
- Specifying criteria for querying model

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714840 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00355: Creating Tags with Default Values

- page_id: `136714840`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714840/Creating+Tags+with+Default+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:52.212+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Working with Profiles > Creating Profiles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a tag with a default value

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024x'] (Property).
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MD2024x'] for this tag.
3. Specify multiplicity larger than 0 (1, 1..*, 2, etc.).

Multiplicity means that this tag is mandatory.

When the stereotype is applied, mandatory tags will be automatically created and default values will be assigned.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Stereotypes' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024x']
