<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712904 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00393: Customizing severity levels

- page_id: `136712904`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712904/Customizing+severity+levels
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:06.429+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules > Specifying the validation rule properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can customize the default severity levels (such as **debug**, **info**, **warning**, **error**, **fatal**) by creating new and and additional levels. For this you must edit the *SeverityKind* [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MD2024x']element, located in the UML Standard Profile.

We do not recommend changing the UML Standard Profile.

To customize severity levels

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x']
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x']
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x']
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x']

After you create the custom severity level or modify the default severity, you can select it from from the drop-down list of the **Severity**property when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the validation rule properties' space='MD2024x']. In the following figure the new severity level named *critical* can be selected as a value of the **Severity** property in the Specification window of validation rule (Constraint element). The value order in the **Severity** drop-down list is the same as they are listed in the *SeverityKind*Enumerationelement.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The custom severity level named critical in the drop-down list of the Severity property in the validation rule specification.

##### editEditing the UML Standard Profile

To edit the UML Standard Profile

1. Open a project.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and enable to show the Show Auxiliary Resources in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] .
3. Right-click the UML Standard Profile Package.
4. Select Project Usages > Open . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024x']>]]> The UML Standard Profile is now editable.

##### createCreating new or modifying default severity levels

You can create new or modify default severity levels only if the UML Standard Profile is editable. To see how to edit the profile read the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x'].

Each Enumeration Literal created for the Enumeration element corresponds to available severity levels.

To modify default severity levels (Enumeration Literals)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package.
2. Double-click the SeverityKind Enumeration element to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
3. On the left side of the Specification window, select the Enumeration Literals [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='default_severity_levels.png']
4. modifyOn the right side of the Specification window, select severity level from the list: 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']. 
 - Click the **Up**/**Down** button to move the severity level up/down in the list. Severity levels are listed in ascending order according to severity importance. - Click the **Clone** button to create the same severity level. The Specification window of newly created Enumeration Literal opens. Type its unique name. 
 - Click the **Delete**button to remove the selected severity level from the model.
5. Click Close . The default severity levels are modified.

To create a new severity level (Enumeration Literal)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package.
2. Double-click the SeverityKind Enumeration element to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
3. On the left side of the Specification window, select the Enumeration Literals [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
4. On the right side of the Specification window, click the Create button. The Specification window of the newly created Enumeration Literal opens.
5. In the Name property, type the name of the new severity level (e.g. critical).
6. Click **Close**. 
The new severity level is created and can be[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### specifyDefining new icon for severity level

You can define a different icon for a custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). For this, you must create a new stereotype and specify its **Icon** and **Metaclass** properties.

To define a new icon for the severity level (Enumeration Literals)

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] , right-click the Validation Profile Package, and select Create Element > Stereotype .
2. Type its name (e.g. criticalIcon) and press Enter .
3. Double-click newly created Stereotype.
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of Stereotype specify the following properties: - Select the Icon property value, click [ATTACHMENT filename='image_library.png'] to select an image from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Image Library tab' space='MD2024x'] , or [ATTACHMENT filename='custom_image.png'] to select an image from your file system. - Select the Metaclass property value, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and select EnumerationLiteral. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_icon_for_severity_level.png']
5. Click Close . The new stereotype with a new icon is created and ready to be applied to a severity level. >]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024x']

##### applyApplying new icon for severity level

After you define the new icon for severity level, you can apply it to custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). This icon is shown when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024x'] of your model.

To apply a new icon to a severity level

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] , click + to expand the UML Standard Profile Package > Validation Profile Package > SeverityKind Enumeration element.
2. Double-click the severity level (Enumeration Literals element) to which you want to apply a new icon.
3. In its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , select the Applied Stereotype property value.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and select the newly created stereotype (e.g. criticalIcon) dedicated for this severity level.
5. Select Apply .
6. Click Close . The new icon for the severity level is applied. In the figure below, the criticalIcon stereotype is applied to the new critical EnumerationLiteral element. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_icon_applied_for_new_severity_level.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1e990b9-2f5d-4cc0-8da3-c53a9d6877e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can customize the default severity levels (such as <strong>debug</strong>, <strong>info</strong>, <strong>warning</strong>, <strong>error</strong>, <strong>fatal</strong>) by creating new and and additional levels. For this you must edit the <em>SeverityKind</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Enumeration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Enumeration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element, located in the UML Standard Profile.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cadc8c8-d89b-4462-89b6-3df3573c91a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not recommend changing the UML Standard Profile.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To customize severity levels</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit the UML Standard Profile.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new or modify default severity levels.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="specify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Define new icon for severity level.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="apply"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Apply new icon for severity level.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link></p></li></ol><p>After you create the custom severity level or modify the default severity, you can select it from from the drop-down list of the <strong>Severity </strong>property when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specifying the validation rule properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specifying the validation rule properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In the following figure the new severity level named <em>critical</em> can be selected as a value of the <strong>Severity</strong> property in the Specification window of  validation rule (Constraint element). The value order in the <strong>Severity</strong> drop-down list is the same as they are listed in the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_severity_level.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The custom severity level named critical in the drop-down list of the Severity property in the validation rule specification.</h6><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88d746be-bf68-4add-b19a-efc25d908fbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">edit</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Editing the UML Standard Profile</h3><p>To edit the UML Standard Profile</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a project.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and enable to show the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Right-click the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Open</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to manage project usages >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />The UML Standard Profile is now editable.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f356329-9909-4f1d-a833-6a8370a78627"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating new or modifying default severity levels</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f75f0bb8-b7ea-43b4-af1e-e687498fc49a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create new or modify default severity levels only if the UML Standard Profile is editable. <span class="confluence-link">To see how to edit the profile read</span> the <ac:link ac:anchor="edit"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[procedure above]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Each Enumeration Literal created for the Enumeration element corresponds to available severity levels.</p><p><br /></p><p>To modify default severity levels (Enumeration Literals)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element to open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>On the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Enumeration Literals</strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ property group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_severity_levels.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028719e0-b30e-44b4-b318-2ce340166151"><ac:parameter ac:name="">modify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>On the right side of the Specification window, select severity level from the list:<br />  - Click <ac:image ac:title="Open Specification" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Open Specification" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.<br />  - Click the <strong>Up</strong>/<strong>Down</strong> button to move the severity level up/down in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ea85a93-0d00-4548-8d50-7655576bb1f1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Severity levels are listed in ascending order according to severity importance.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">  - Click the <strong>Clone</strong> button to create the same severity level. The Specification window of newly created Enumeration Literal opens. Type its unique name.<br />  - Click the <strong>Delete </strong>button to remove the selected severity level from the model.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The default severity levels are modified.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To create a new severity level (Enumeration Literal)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element to open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>On the left side of the Specification window, select the <strong>Enumeration Literals</strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ property group]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>On the right side of the Specification window, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The Specification window of the newly created Enumeration Literal opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Name</strong> property, type the name of the new severity level (e.g. critical).</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new severity level is created and can be<ac:link ac:anchor="modify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ modified by using buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_severity_level_in_the_specification_of_enumeration_SeverityKind.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e13b5d38-57f0-462a-8f5b-5a272aa7c4ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Defining new icon for severity level</h3><p>You can define a different icon for a custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). For this, you must create a new stereotype and specify its <strong>Icon</strong> and <strong>Metaclass</strong> properties.</p><p>To define a new icon for the severity level (Enumeration Literals)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click the<em> Validation Profile</em> Package, and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Stereotype</strong>.</li><li>Type its name (e.g. criticalIcon) and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click newly created Stereotype.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of Stereotype specify the following properties:<br />  - Select the <strong>Icon</strong> property value, click <ac:image ac:title="Image Library" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Image Library" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_library.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to select an image from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Image Library tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Image Library]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:image ac:title="Custom Image" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Custom Image" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_image.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to select an image from your file system.<br />  - Select the <strong>Metaclass</strong> property value, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select EnumerationLiteral.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_icon_for_severity_level.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new stereotype with a new icon is created and ready to be applied to a severity level. <ac:link ac:anchor="apply"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to apply it >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ac:link></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aae655a1-0d9a-4276-9e43-3e86888e4b9c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">apply</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Applying new icon for severity level</h3><p>After you define the new icon for severity level, you can apply it to custom or default severity levels (Enumeration Literals). This icon is shown when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[representing invalid elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of your model.</p><p>To apply a new icon to a severity level</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click + to expand the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>Validation Profile</em> Package &gt; <em>SeverityKind</em><span class="confluence-link"> Enumeration </span>element.</li><li class="confluence-link">Double-click the <span class="confluence-link">severity level (Enumeration Literals element) to </span>which you want to apply a new icon.</li><li class="confluence-link">In its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, select the <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong> property value.</li><li class="confluence-link">Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the newly created stereotype (e.g. criticalIcon) dedicated for this severity level.</li><li class="confluence-link">Select <strong>Apply</strong>.</li><li class="confluence-link">Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The new icon for the severity level is applied. In the figure below, the <em>criticalIcon</em> stereotype is applied to the new <em>critical</em> EnumerationLiteral element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_icon_applied_for_new_severity_level.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714940 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00394: Customizing Specification Window

- page_id: `136714940`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714940/Customizing+Specification+Window
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:46:53.881+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main purpose of DSL is to hide UML, so that DSL elements would look standard or as new type elements in models. DSL element properties are specified in the DSL element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] and the **Properties** panel (at the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='']) as regular properties.

There are several customizations and grouping cases for properties:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The main purpose of DSL is to hide UML, so that DSL elements would look standard or as new type elements in models. </span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">DSL element properties are specified in the DSL element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> and the <strong>Properties</strong> panel (at the bottom of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Browser window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) as regular properties. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">There are several customizations and grouping cases for properties:</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ee98e028-daea-49b3-b084-e4c5b83ff849" /><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711681 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00395: Customizing stereotype style properties

- page_id: `136711681`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711681/Customizing+stereotype+style+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:49.906+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Stereotype properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize properties like colors, line styles, and line width, for your stereotypes. To be able to do it, you need to:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype properties' space='MD2024x']
2. Once the stereotype is added, customize stereotype style properties either via the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']** or **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']** dialog.

In the**Project Options** dialog, after specifying the stereotype style properties, click**Apply** to apply changes to existing shapes or the **OK** button to apply changes to the new shapes only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing stereotype style properties using the Project Options dialog

In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, click**Make Default** after setting the desired style properties for your stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Customizing stereotype style properties using the Symbol Properties dialog

The best practice is to create styles for stereotypes in the Profile, loading it as a project. When a project uses the Profile, styles for stereotypes will also be loaded and used. These styles will be applied right after applying the stereotype to an element and will be used in all diagrams.

Style for stereotype in custom diagram

- The custom diagram wizard allows specifying styles for stereotyped elements.

stereotype to the Stereotypes option group.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><p class="_mce_tagged_br">Once the stereotype is added, customize stereotype style properties either via the dialog.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ec8a5cf-e0da-4f76-b655-ead647229c1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712985 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00396: Customizing suspect links tracking

- page_id: `136712985`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712985/Customizing+suspect+links+tracking
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:12.444+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Suspect Links
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1398447377

1398447388

1398447375

**On this page**

**53**

1398447378

In addition to tracking the changes directly affecting Requirements linked to other model elements, you can create your own custom suspect link configurations to keep track of the changes applied to selected model elements and their properties at the chosen end (source or target) of the specified dependencies.

A newly created suspect links configuration comes into effect immediately and overrides a configuration previously used in the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Suspect links tracking in the model

##### Customizing tracking

To create a custom suspect link configuration

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] , right-click a package to create your configuration in, and select Create Element > Class . Name it.
2. Right-click the configuration [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'], select**Stereotype,**and apply the«SuspectLinkConfiguration»stereotype. To apply the «SuspectLinkConfiguration» stereotype, suspect links tracking must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space='MD2024x'] in your project (**Analyze** > **Suspect Links** > **Enable Tracking**).If you use Cameo Systems Modeler, create a Block instead of a Class in step 1, remove the «Block» stereotype from the Block, then apply the«SuspectLinkConfiguration»stereotype to that Block.
3. Open the created element's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
4. Specify the following [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing suspect links tracking' space='MD2024x'] :
  - Tracked Dependency Type
  - Tracked End
  - Tracked Element Type
  - Tracked Element Properties
5. Close the window when you are done and press F5 to refresh your project. 
The configuration is created. Please note that it is not automatically applied to relationships that existed in the model prior to creating a configuration for suspect links tracking. In such a case, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes' space='MD2024x'] links manually.

##### Suspect Links configuration propertiesConfiguration properties

These are the propertiesyou need to specify for your custom suspect linkconfiguration:

| Property name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Tracked Dependency Type | Specify the types of dependencies to keep track of their source/target element changes. |
| Tracked End | Specify whether the source or the target element of the selected dependencies should be tracked. The target end-related changes are tracked by default. |
| Tracked Element Type | Specify the element types to keep track of their changes. |
| Tracked Element Properties | Specify the properties of the selected model elements to keep track of their changes. If left empty, all element properties are tracked by default. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fbdd481b-d03b-4756-87df-660ce08813b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1398447377</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cdd6b53a-4e84-416f-be44-b48b58877b2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1398447388</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88daf843-d947-47c7-9b6f-d5dc291dd7b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1398447375</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab6cd48b-ba5c-47d6-b31e-687d487cc265"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ff88d6f-cafa-422d-bb49-5638aee2349c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1398447378</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b2eba893-0ba5-40ec-9020-3027f36dfba1">In addition to tracking the changes directly affecting Requirements linked to other model elements, you can create your own custom suspect link configurations to keep track of the changes applied to selected model elements and their properties at the chosen end (source or target) of the specified dependencies. </ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfa80639-b247-4ae5-b245-f5a5ff607605"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A newly created suspect <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e3a18cfc-d0b1-4d41-80e9-cf75380506d4">links</ac:inline-comment-marker> configuration comes into effect immediately and overrides a configuration previously used in the project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="suspect_links (2).png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing suspect links tracking" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Suspect links tracking in the model</span></h6><h3>Customizing tracking</h3><p>To create <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03247926-b1d4-4efc-a50a-04b83dfa513d">a custom <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9b4b04c3-174a-49ec-86e3-2a640fed852a">suspect link</ac:inline-comment-marker> configuration</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, right-click a package to create your configuration in, and select <strong>Create Element </strong>&gt;<strong> Class</strong>. Name it.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the configuration <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Stereotype,</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and apply the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">SuspectLinkConfiguration</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">»</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> stereotype.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7097fca7-f0ff-4fa7-a41c-a4381bff3069"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To apply the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«</span>SuspectLinkConfiguration<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">»</span> stereotype, suspect links tracking must be <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your project (<strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Suspect Links</strong> &gt; <strong>Enable Tracking</strong>).</li><li>If you use Cameo Systems Modeler, create a Block instead of a Class in step 1, remove the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«</span>Block<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">»</span> stereotype from the Block, then apply <span>the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">«</span><span>SuspectLinkConfiguration</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">»</span><span> stereotype to that Block.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Open the created element's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="40668c93-4092-4f14-9460-e8b14bdced70">following </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:link ac:anchor="Suspect Links configuration properties"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[configuration properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing suspect links tracking" /></ac:link>:<ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Tracked Dependency Type</strong></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Tracked End</strong></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Tracked Element Type</strong></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Tracked Element Properties</strong><strong> </strong></li></ul></li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fc1057a2-8831-469f-90c2-6f1668618e3f">Close the window when you are done and press F5 to refresh your project.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br />The configuration is created. Please note that it is not automatically applied to relationships that existed in the model <span>prior to creating a configuration for suspect links tracking. In such a case, you need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Solving untracked links issue"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Tracking elements affected by Requirement changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[start tracking]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> links manually. </span></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fb925c4-de00-4013-bb38-ecfdcc7fdbfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Suspect Links configuration properties</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Configuration properties</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a243d291-51bf-471e-94f6-d6291bf8c92d">These are the properties</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> you need to specify for your custom suspect link </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f3899902-2140-4cb8-a4a9-a9ebdf960a10">configuration</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">:</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 59.8913%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 27.4296%;" /><col style="width: 72.5704%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Tracked Dependency Type</strong></td><td>Specify the types of dependencies to keep track of their source/target element changes.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Tracked End</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Specify whether the source or the target element of the selected dependencies should be tracked. The target end-related changes are tracked by default. </span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tracked Element Type</strong></td><td>Specify the element types to keep track of their changes.</td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tracked Element Properties</strong></p></td><td><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fad20b55-a0ba-4081-9bd8-0ce9d4ed8ce4">Specify the properties of the selected model elements to keep track of their changes. If left empty, all element properties are tracked by default.</ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target" /></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714992 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00397: Customizing Symbols

- page_id: `136714992`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714992/Customizing+Symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:59.769+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Setting default symbol size

The **Default Shape Size** property defines the default size of a shape when you create a symbol in a diagram. Use the **Default Shape Size**property in the Customization element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] to define the default symbol size in DSL customization:

- The first value is the width (x) in pixels. Value= 0 is used if a default symbol width should stay, but the height should change.
- The second value is a height (y) in pixels.

A default shape size is used everywhere a new symbol is created - using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Drag-and-Drop' space='MD2024x'], diagram toolbar button, diagram wizards, etc.

###### Setting custom path style

Customize the custom path style by changing:

- Line style (dashes, dots, solid line, etc).
- Arrow style at the first end and arrow style at the second end.
- Custom scalable icon for the first end and for the second style.

To create the custom path style

1. In a profile, create a stereotype.
2. Right-click either the stereotype or a created stereotype (usually created in the profile diagram). Select Metaclass . In the Select Metaclass dialog, clear all selections. Select the Relationships metaclass.
3. Customize the path style. In the stereotype Specification window, click the Icon property and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button.
4. Click Yes to open the Path Icon Customization dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='path_icon_customization_dialog.png']
5. Specify the path style.
6. Click OK when you are done.
7. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'] to a relationship.

#### INFO: Symbol Properties

Symbol Properties

Change the line width and the rest of the path properties in the [**Symbol Properties**dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Symbol+Properties+dialog).

Path icon usage rules:

- Custom appearance is used only after applying the stereotype.
- End icons are rotated according to path position and direction.
- The end icon size is scaled according to font size (as with standard arrows).

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

44

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties Description' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715326 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00398: Customizing templates

- page_id: `136715326`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715326/Customizing+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:22.655+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change the generation of the particular Oracle Object, add a new functionality to its macro in the velocity template. The Helper utility class will assist in retrieving model information.

We suggest to make a back up of the default template. The default template is stored in <*program installation folder>\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template*folder. The template file can be changed in the **CG Properties Editor**dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the **Properties**command).

When generating DDL, objects are passed from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code engineering set' space='MD2024x'] to the velocity engine. Objects, that are passed to the Oracle DDL velocity engine are presented in the following table:

| Object name | Object type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| $CESList | List<NamedEleme nt> | The list of model elements that are added to the . |
| $dropRequired | boolean | Flag to notify if the drop objects is required. This is a drop setting option which generates the Drop statement. |
| $oracleHelper | Java.lang.Class | This is a helper class for retrieving information from model elements. Use this class to check the element for applied stereotypes, get tagged values and element’s owned information. |
| $newLineBracket | boolean | The generation Option to generate a bracket in a new line. |
| $genDocumentation | boolean | The language option for the documentation generation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To change the generation of the particular Oracle Object, add a new functionality to its macro in the velocity template. The Helper utility class will assist in retrieving model information. </p><p>We suggest to make a back up of the default template. The default template is stored in &lt;<em>program installation folder&gt;\ data\DB_engineering\Oracle_template </em>folder. The template file can be changed in the <strong>CG Properties Editor </strong>dialog box (in the Oracle DDL set shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Properties </strong>command).</p><p>When generating DDL, objects are passed from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the velocity engine. Objects, that are passed to the Oracle DDL velocity engine are presented in the following table:</p><table><tbody><tr><th><strong>Object name</strong></th><th><strong>Object type</strong></th><th><strong>Description</strong></th></tr><tr><td><pre>$CESList</pre></td><td><pre>List&lt;NamedEleme nt&gt;</pre></td><td><span>The list of model elements that are added to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Code engineering set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[code engineering set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$dropRequired</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>Flag to notify if the drop objects is required. This is a drop setting option which generates the Drop statement.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$oracleHelper</pre></td><td><pre>Java.lang.Class</pre></td><td><span>This is a helper class for retrieving information from model elements. Use this class to check the element for applied stereotypes, get tagged values and element’s owned information.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$newLineBracket</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>The generation Option to generate a bracket in a new line.</span></td></tr><tr><td><pre>$genDocumentation</pre></td><td><pre>boolean</pre></td><td><span>The language option for the documentation generation.</span></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709372 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00399: Customizing the conversion options

- page_id: `136709372`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709372/Customizing+the+conversion+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:46.184+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Working with Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to the options that you have in the **Import Enterprise Architect File**dialog, several other conversion options exist in the modeling tool, and you can specify it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024x']**Environment Options**dialog.

To customize Enterprise Architect Import options in the **Environment Options**dialog

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment to open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the options group list, select Enterprise Architect Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='Environment Options.png']
3. The transformation options are classified into two groups: General and Activity element mapping :
  - Options in the General group are the same as those in the Import Enterprise Architect Files dialog.
  - The Activity element mapping group provides options to convert the EA elements to other element types as it is described in the following table:

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Convert EA WriteVariableAction to | To convert EA WriteVariableAction to either AddVariableValueAction or RemoveVariableValueAction. |
| Convert EA WriteLinkAction to | To convert EA WriteLinkAction to either CreateLinkAction or DestroyLinkAction. |
| Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to | To convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to either AddStructuralFeatureValueAction or RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In addition to the options that you have in the <strong>Import Enterprise Architect File </strong>dialog, several other conversion options exist in the modeling tool, and you can specify it in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize Enterprise Architect Import options in the <strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Environment </strong>to open the <strong>Environment </strong><strong>Options </strong>dialog.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>Enterprise Architect Import</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Environment Options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing the conversion options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The transformation options are classified into two groups: <strong>General</strong> and <strong>Activity element mapping</strong>:<br /><ul><li>Options in the <strong>General </strong>group are the same as those in the <strong>Import Enterprise Architect Files </strong>dialog.</li><li>The <strong>Activity element mapping</strong> group provides options to convert the EA elements to other element types as it is described in the following table:<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Convert EA WriteVariableAction to</strong></p></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteVariableAction to either AddVariableValueAction or RemoveVariableValueAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Convert EA WriteLinkAction to</strong></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteLinkAction to either CreateLinkAction or DestroyLinkAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to</strong></p></td><td><p>To convert EA WriteStructuralFeatureAction to either AddStructuralFeatureValueAction or RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709958 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00400: Cutting element names

- page_id: `136709958`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709958/Cutting+element+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:17.972+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-cut-element-name']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The element names can be cut if they are too long, which makes the relation map difficult to read.

To cut element names

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. Select the Cut Element Names option.

To see the entire name of an element, move the pointer over the element name. A tooltip showing the entire name appears.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The element names can be cut if they are too long, which makes the relation map difficult to read.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b0a93e26-c193-4133-bace-ef02f45419b9">To cut element names</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>Select the <strong>Cut Element Names </strong>option.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f601b47d-de99-4300-843a-0078e572e13f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To see the entire name of an element, move the pointer over the element name. A tooltip showing the entire name appears.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cut_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Cutting element names" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714069 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00401: Data file

- page_id: `136714069`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714069/Data+file
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:06.326+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Template Editor allows you to create a new data file and import it to the Report Wizard Template Editor dialog. Click the Import button to import the data file. The data file is written in the *XML* format. The following is the example of an *XML* data file schema, see the code snippet below.

```text
]]>
```

The table below lists the XML Data File Schema Elements and Their Descriptions

| Element | Attribute / Element | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| data |  | A root element. |
| name : string | A data name. This value will be used as a category name in (3) Category. |  |
| elements : complexType | A group of <element> elements. |  |
| elements |  | A group of <element> elements. |
| element |  | An element display on (4) List of fields. |
|  | name : string | An element’s name. |
|  | description : string | An element description. |
|  | often : boolean | If an attribute is true, the element will be managed as a suggested element. |
|  | members : complexType | A group of <function> or <attribute>. |
| function |  | A function member. |
|  | name : string | A function’s name. |
|  | description : string | A function description. |
|  | param : complexType | A group of function parameters. |
| param |  | A function parameter. |
|  | name : string | A parameter’s name. |
|  | description : string | A parameter description. |
|  | type : string | A parameter type. |
|  | direction : simpleType | The direction of a parameter:in indicates this parameter is an input.outindicates this parameter is an output. |
| attribute |  | An attribute member. |
|  | name : string | An attribute’s name. |
|  | description : string | An attribute description. |
|  | type : string | An attribute type. |

The following code snippet is an example of an XML data file.

```text
array
      Use for creating the Array or HashSet instance.
      
        
          subList
          Create ArrayList of the portion of list in given size.
          
            list
            in
            List
            a original list.
          
          
            size
            in
            int
            view size of given list
          
          
            list
            out
            
            a of view of the specified size within given list.
          
        
      
    
  
]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Template Editor allows you to create a new data file and import it to the Report Wizard Template Editor dialog. Click the Import button to import the data file. The data file is written in the <em>XML</em> format. The following is the example of an <em>XML</em> data file schema, see the code snippet below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfb09981-ad79-417e-a30b-ebf4dc100ed4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<xs:schema elementFormDefault="qualified" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">
  <xs:element name="data">
   <xs:complexType>
    <xs:sequence>
     <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="elements">
      <xs:complexType>
       <xs:sequence>
        <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="element">
         <xs:complexType>
          <xs:all>
           <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
           <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
           <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="members">
            <xs:complexType>
             <xs:sequence>
             <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="function">
              <xs:complexType>
               <xs:sequence>
                <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
                <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
                <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="param">
                 <xs:complexType>
                  <xs:all>
                   <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
                   <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
                   <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="type" nillable="true" type="xs:string" />
                   <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="direction">
                    <xs:simpleType>
                     <xs:restriction base="xs:string">
                      <xs:enumeration value="in" />
                      <xs:enumeration value="out" />
                     </xs:restriction>
                    </xs:simpleType>
                   </xs:element>
                  </xs:all>
                 </xs:complexType>
                </xs:element>
               </xs:sequence>
              </xs:complexType>
             </xs:element>
              <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded" name="attribute">
               <xs:complexType>
                <xs:all>
                 <xs:element minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="1" name="name" type="xs:string" />
                 <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="description" type="xs:string" />
                 <xs:element minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="1" name="type" nillable="true" type="xs:string" />
                </xs:all>
               </xs:complexType>
              </xs:element>
             </xs:sequence>
            </xs:complexType>
           </xs:element>
          </xs:all>
          <xs:attribute name="often" type="xs:boolean" />
         </xs:complexType>
        </xs:element>
       </xs:sequence>
      </xs:complexType>
     </xs:element>
   </xs:sequence>
   <xs:attribute name="name" type="xs:string" />
  </xs:complexType>
 </xs:element>
</xs:schema>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The table below lists the XML Data File Schema Elements and Their Descriptions</p><table><tbody><tr><th>Element </th><th>Attribute / Element </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"> data  </td><td> </td><td>A root element.</td></tr><tr><td>name : string</td><td>A data name. This <br />value will be used as <br />a category name in (3) <br />Category. </td></tr><tr><td>elements : complexType</td><td>A group of &lt;element&gt; <br />elements. </td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">elements</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">A group of &lt;element&gt; <br />elements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">element</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">An element display on (4) <br />List of fields.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">An element’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">An element description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">often : boolean</td><td colspan="1">If an attribute is true, the <br />element will be managed <br />as a suggested element.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">members : complexType</td><td colspan="1">A group of &lt;function&gt; or <br />&lt;attribute&gt;.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">function</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">A function member.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">A function’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">A function description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">param : complexType</td><td colspan="1">A group of function <br />parameters.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">param</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">A function parameter.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">A parameter’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">A parameter description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">type : string</td><td colspan="1">A parameter type.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">direction : simpleType</td><td colspan="1"><p>The direction of a parameter:</p><ul><li>in <br />indicates this parameter is an input.</li><li>out<br />indicates this parameter is an output.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">attribute</td><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">An attribute member.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">name : string</td><td colspan="1">An attribute’s name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">description : string</td><td colspan="1">An attribute description.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"> </td><td colspan="1">type : string</td><td colspan="1">An attribute type.</td></tr></tbody></table><p> <br /> The following code snippet is an example of an XML data file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4294de46-0f48-44ed-a51b-cb9564e16a84"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<data name="Built-in Tools" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchemainstance"
xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="fields.xsd">
  <elements>
    <element often="true">
      <name>array</name>
      <description>Use for creating the Array or HashSet instance.</description>
      <members>
        <function>
          <name>subList</name>
          <description>Create ArrayList of the portion of list in given size.</description>
          <param>
            <name>list</name>
            <direction>in</direction>
            <type>List</type>
            <description>a original list.</description>
          </param>
          <param>
            <name>size</name>
            <direction>in</direction>
            <type>int</type>
            <description>view size of given list</description>
          </param>
          <param>
            <name>list</name>
            <direction>out</direction>
            <type/>
            <description>a of view of the specified size within given list.</description>
          </param>
        </function>
      </members>
    </element>
  </elements>
</data>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711211 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00402: Data Store Node

- page_id: `136711211`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711211/Data+Store+Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:27.642+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Data Store Node keeps all tokens that enter it, copying them when they are chosen to be moved downstream. Incoming tokens containing a particular object replace any tokens in the object node that contains the particular object.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Data Store Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the Object Node icon and select **Data Store Node** from the shortcut menu . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Data Store Node is created.
3. (Optional) To specify the Data Store Node by changing its property values, right-click its symbol and open its Specification window .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Data Store Node keeps all tokens that enter it, copying them when they are chosen to be moved downstream. Incoming tokens containing a particular object replace any tokens in the object node that contains the particular object.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Data_store_node.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Data Store Node" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To create a Data Store Node </p><hr /><ol><li>Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the <strong>Object Node</strong><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">icon and select <strong>Data Store Node</strong> from the shortcut menu</span>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_data_store_node.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Data Store Node" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Data Store Node<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span>is created. </li><li>(Optional) <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To specify the Data Store Node by changing its property values, right-click its symbol and open its </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710986 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00403: Data Type

- page_id: `136710986`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710986/Data+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:10.436+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Data Type is a type whose values have no identity; that is, they are pure values. It is a classifier and inherits the general features of the classifier: visibility, generalizable element properties, and operations.

MagicDraw provides the following predefined data types: boolean, byte, char, date, double, float, int, Integer, Real, long, short, void, and String.

You can also create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='MD2024x']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Primitive' space='MD2024x']Data Types.

To create a new Data Type, including Enumeration or Primitive

- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] , select New from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] or Create Element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsystem' space='MD2024x'] , or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model' space='MD2024x'] shortcut menu, and then select Data Type, Enumeration, or Primitive.
- In the Class, Package, Subsystem, and Model [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , the Inner Elements property group, click Create and select a Data Type.

A Data Type can be specified by changing its property values in the Data Type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']

Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3eecbb8-7b24-4e78-a0fb-5c818aadc291"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d73cd36e-f4ee-4087-888b-57f1c9aed597"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00404: Data Type From Old Project Versions

- page_id: `136715588`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715588/Data+Type+From+Old+Project+Versions

1398641584

1398641587

1398641586

##### bool

There are five cases of using the bool data type: Attribute type, Parameter type, Return type, Actual in Template Parameter Substitution and Default in Classifier Template Parameter. Our modeling tool will change the old bool data type to new bool data type in a new profile.

**Attribute type**

The old bool data type in an Attribute type is shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile).

**Parameter type**

The old and new bool data types in the Parameter type are shown in the figures below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile).

**Return type**

The old bool data type in the Return type is shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile).

**Actual in Template Parameter Substitution**

The old bool data type in the Actual in Template Parameter Substitution is shown in the figure below.

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile). | Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile). |

**Default in Classifier Template Parameter**

The old bool data type in the Default in Classifier Template Parameter is shown in the figure below.

| Old value | Translation |
| --- | --- |
| Old bool data type in old profile (C++ Profile). | Change to the new bool data type in new profile (c++ ANSI profile). |

1398641583

## PAGE 00405: Date tool

- page_id: `136714025`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714025/Date+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:01.675+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2049623106

2049623118

2049623105

**On this page**

43

2049623108

$date is a method to display and format the current date and time.

###### **$date**

Display the current date and time, for example, Oct 19, 2003 and 9:54:50 PM respectively.

###### **$date.long**

Display the current date and time in a long form, for example, October 19, 2003 and 9:54:50 PM. respectively.

###### **$date.full_date**

Display the current date in a full form, for example, Sunday, October 19, 2003.

###### **$date.get('format')**

Display the current date in a specific format, for example, $date.get('yyyy-M-d H:m:s') is displayed as 2003-10-19 21:54:50.

| Letter | Date or Time Component | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| G | Era designator | AD |
| y | Year 1996 | 96 |
| M | Month in year | July; Jul; 07 |
| w | Week in year | 27 |
| W | Week in month | 2 |
| D | Day in year | 189 |
| d | Day in month | 10 |
| F | Day of week in month | 2 |
| E | Day in week | Tuesday; Tue |
| a | Am/pm marker | PM |
| H | Hour in day (0-23) | 0 |
| k | Hour in day (1-24) | 24 |
| K | Hour in am/pm (0-11) | 0 |
| h | Hour in am/pm (1-12) | 12 |
| m | Minute in hour | 30 |
| s | Second in minute | 55 |
| S | Millisecond | 978 |
| z | Time zone | Pacific Standard Time; PST; GMT-08.00 |
| Z | Time zone | Time zone -0800 |

###### **Year**

- If the number of letters in a pattern letters is four or more, a calendar specific long form is used.
- Otherwise, a calendar specific short or abbreviated form is used.

| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| yy | 10 |
| yyyy | 2010 |

###### **Month**

- If the number of letters in a pattern is less than 3, the number form is used.
- If the number of letters in a pattern is 3, a short or abbreviated form is used.
- If the number of letters in a pattern is 4 or more, the full form is used.

| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| M | 4 |
| MM | 04 |
| MMM | Apr |
| MMMM | April |

###### **Days**

- If the number of letters in a pattern is four or more, the full form is used.
- Otherwise, a calendar specific short or abbreviated form is used.

| Date and Time Pattern | Result |
| --- | --- |
| E | Mon |
| EEEE |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714376 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00406: Debug report template

- page_id: `136714376`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714376/Debug+report+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:27.388+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1336400640

1336400641

1336400641

The Debug report template features warning messages that describe template errors. Warning messages appear in a Message dialog or in the terminal while the report template is being processed in MagicDraw UI or command line. Warning messages are intended only for the informative purpose and have no effect on output reports.

The warning message types are as follows:

- Invalid Property
- Invalid Reference
- Invalid Method Reference
- Exception
- Invalid Syntax

1336400640

**Related pages**

Warning messages appear in a Message dialog or in the terminal while the report template is being processed in MagicDraw UI or command line. Warning messages are intended only for the informative purpose and have no effect on output reports.</p><p>The warning message types are as follows:</p><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid Property</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid Reference</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid Method Reference</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Exception</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Invalid Syntax</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="529fbe08-f49e-44b2-af0b-79f0afaeba8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336400640</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9f2fa4ed-9dc6-4c30-9bbb-6a28efa0a5fe" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710989 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00407: Decision and Merge

- page_id: `136710989`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710989/Decision+and+Merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:11.001+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['decision', 'merge']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943280858

943280861

943280859

A Decision Node in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x'] is used much like a choice or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Junction' space='MD2024x'] point in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']. Decision nodes allow you to separate the Activity Edges. Merge nodes allow you to combine the Activity Edges together.

Decision Nodes are created using guard conditions. They help protect Activity Edges that depend on guard conditions.

1. The symbol used for the Decision Node is a large diamond shape. It can have one or two incoming Activity Edges, and at least one outgoing Activity Edge
2. The symbol used for the Merge Node is a large diamond shape. It can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges.

The Decision and Merge are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the **Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge****notations** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='MD2024x'] to draw different notations for the**Decision**and**Merge**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activity diagram illustrates that when an order cancellation request is made (only while filling, receiving or shipping orders) - the Cancel Order behavior is invoked.

943280856

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='MD2024x']
- Activity diagram
- Junction
- Model Elements

Decision nodes allow you to separate the Activity Edges. Merge nodes allow you to combine the Activity Edges together. </p><p>Decision Nodes are created using guard conditions. They help protect Activity Edges that depend on guard conditions.</p><ol><li>The symbol used for the Decision Node is a large diamond shape. It can have one or two incoming Activity Edges, and at least one outgoing Activity Edge</li><li>The symbol used for the Merge Node is a large diamond shape. It can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges.</li></ol><p>The Decision and Merge are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the <strong>Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge</strong><span><strong> notations</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to draw different notations for the </span><strong>Decision</strong><span> and </span><strong>Merge</strong><span>.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="decision-merge.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Decision and Merge" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activity diagram illustrates that when an order cancellation request is made (only while filling, receiving or shipping orders) - the Cancel Order behavior is invoked.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="874d329f-6bcc-4364-b901-b6155101dd17"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943280856</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Activity+diagram">Activity diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Junction">Junction</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
